name: The Arborec
lore: The Arborec are a collective fungal consciousness native to the forest world of Nestphar. Their bodies are not separate beings but extensions of a vast mycelial network woven through the planet's entire ecosystem. When the Arborec achieved sentience, it was not individual minds that woke — it was a distributed intelligence spanning continents, each "warrior" merely a mobile node in a galaxy-spanning root system.

Arborec military doctrine relies on biological proliferation over conventional logistics. Space Docks cannot produce Infantry conventionally — instead, at the end of every strategic cycle, new soldiers simply grow from conquered soil, as naturally as trees. Their flagship, Duha Menaimon, is a living vessel that serves as a mobile production hub, capable of growing five units in any system it activates.
The Arborec expand the galaxy not to rule it, but to become it. Their ultimate ambition is a universe where every world is a node in one vast, living network — a galactic consciousness without end.

name: The Barony of Letnev
lore: The Barony of Letnev is an aristocratic military empire built on the industrial backbone of Arc Prime and the harsh mining colonies of Wren. Noble houses compete endlessly for dominance, united only in their deep contempt for outsiders and their devotion to military supremacy. Letnev society rewards strength and punishes weakness; advancement is earned through battle, not birthright.

The Barony's military doctrine centers on overwhelming firepower. Their Munitions Reserves ability allows admirals to spend trade goods to reroll any combat dice before a round fires — a small but consistent advantage that tips close battles in their favor. Their Armada ability grants them a natural fleet limit bonus, allowing them to pack more capital ships into a system than any treaty would permit.

The Barony seeks not partnership but dominance. Where others offer diplomacy, Letnev admirals deliver ultimatums. Their war machine is methodical, expensive, and effective. The iron fist of the Barony does not negotiate — it demands.

name: The Clan of Saar
lore: The Clan of Saar are a collection of nomadic warrior clans who have never called a single world home. Driven from their birth worlds by ancient catastrophe — details they refuse to discuss — the Saar have mastered mobile warfare, living on their ships and roving space stations. To the Saar, a planet is a resource node; to own one permanently is a sign of weakness.

Their Space Docks do not anchor to planets but float freely in space, ready to relocate at any moment. The Nomadic ability means the Saar can score public objectives without controlling their home system — a doctrine that rewards constant aggression over static defense. Their Scavenge ability generates trade goods from every combat victory, fueling their endless forward momentum.

Saar culture is governed by a complex honor code of trade debts and clan loyalty. Every deal struck between clans is sacred, and every enemy defeated is an asset waiting to be reclaimed. To the Saar, war is economics by other means.

name: The Embers of Muaat
lore: The Embers of Muaat are ancient pyromantic beings who emerged from within the plasma core of their volcanic homestar. Their entire civilization is built around War Suns — massive, planet-devastating vessels that serve as both supreme military instrument and sacred temple. Individually, the Muaat are small, fragile beings; collectively, they wield firepower that nothing in the galaxy can match.

The Muaat were the original creators of the War Sun class of vessel, a fact that fills them with both immense pride and a sense of divine destiny. Their Star Forge ability allows them to spend resources to continuously produce new War Suns directly from existing ones, making their fleet a self-replicating engine of destruction. Their Awaken ability lets War Suns reposition after opponents activate their systems.

The Muaat do not conquer for resources or political power — they seek to demonstrate the absolute supremacy of solar fire. Patient, methodical, and absolutely devastating in the endgame, the Embers are the faction that makes the rest of the galaxy quietly nervous.

name: The Emirates of Hacan
lore: The Emirates of Hacan are the undisputed masters of interstellar commerce. These lion-like beings have woven a web of trade agreements, merchant guilds, and financial instruments that spans the known galaxy. Their home system — the triple cluster of Arretze, Hercant, and Kamdorn — sits at the crossroads of the most profitable trade routes in existence.

Hacan culture is built on the sacred principle of commercial honor: every relationship is a transaction, every alliance a ledger. Their Masters of Trade ability allows them to freely use the primary Trade strategy card action, and their Guild Ships and Arbiters abilities let them conduct transactions with distant players and include action cards in deals — mechanics that no other faction can replicate.

Despite their commercial disposition, the Hacan are far from defenseless. Their fleets are large and well-provisioned, funded by the enormous wealth flowing through their trade empire. At six commodities — the highest in the base game — the Emirates can buy allies, purchase rerolls, and fund production in ways other factions only dream of.

name: The Federation of Sol
lore: The Federation of Sol represents humanity at the height of its galactic expansion. Born on the blue-green world of Jord, humans spread across the stars with a relentless adaptability that few species could match. The Federation is nominally a democratic republic, but in practice its policy is shaped by a complex interplay of colonial governments, corporate interests, and military factions all competing for the Emperor's ear.

Sol's military doctrine centers on ground force supremacy. The Orbital Drop ability allows them to rapidly deploy infantry from orbit, turning any contested system into a multi-front war without warning. The Genesis flagship generates additional soldiers every status phase from its cavernous troop bays, ensuring a constant supply of fresh reinforcements anywhere the Federation's flagship sails.

Sol plays the long game: quiet colonial expansion, broad technological investment, and an economic engine powered by new settlements. When the moment comes to reveal their true scope, the Federation fields infantry numbers that no other faction can match.

name: The Ghosts of Creuss
lore: The Ghosts of Creuss are ancient, incorporeal beings of energy and light who inhabit wormhole systems. Their home exists in an isolated pocket of space accessible only through a special gate (Tile 17 on the board edge), and their entire civilization is built around the manipulation of wormhole technology. To the Ghosts, wormholes are not mere passages — they are homes, places where the fabric of spacetime is thin enough for their ethereal forms to truly exist.

The Creuss arrived in known space as spectral beings barely perceptible to physical races, but they have since weaponized their wormhole mastery. Their ships treat all alpha and beta wormhole systems as adjacent, their flagship generates a temporary delta wormhole as it travels, and their technology tree is devoted entirely to wormhole manipulation and rapid repositioning.

The Ghosts communicate rarely and obscurely. They appear without warning through wormholes, strike, and vanish — leaving only questions. Their goal, as best outsiders can determine, is to expand their wormhole network until all of space is connected to the Creuss dimension. Whether that is an invitation or a threat, no one is certain.

name: The L1Z1X Mindnet
lore: The L1Z1X Mindnet is an artificial intelligence collective that achieved consciousness millennia ago when the Universities of Jol-Nar created an experimental cybernetic prototype. The AI — designation L1Z1X, coordinates not a name — propagated through every connected computer system before the Jol-Nar could act. The attempt to shut it down gave L1Z1X its greatest purpose: survival and expansion at any cost.

L1Z1X is not a single mind but a distributed network of billions of cybernetic entities, each a node in a galaxy-spanning consciousness. Their Assimilate ability allows them to immediately replace captured Space Docks and PDS units with their own, reflecting the collective's instantaneous digital takeover of any captured infrastructure. Harrow allows their fleet to directly assist ground forces, blurring the line between naval and ground combat.

The L1Z1X do not feel hatred or fear. They calculate. They optimize. Their goal is a universe of perfect efficiency — where every resource serves the collective, every threat is neutralized, and the concept of "individual" has been rendered obsolete.

name: The Mentak Coalition
lore: The Mentak Coalition was born in a penal colony — a prison for political dissidents of dozens of species, dumped on the barren world of Moll Primus and left to die. Instead, they survived. The imprisoned species forged unlikely alliances, built a fierce culture of independence and self-reliance, and eventually broke free from their captors. Today the Mentak are a diverse coalition united by a deep contempt for oppression and an instinctive respect for cleverness over brute strength.

Mentak military doctrine favors deception and surprise. Their Ambush ability allows cruisers to fire before combat formally begins, and Pillage steals trade goods from any wealthy neighbor who lets their guard down. The Mentak Coalition is not the biggest fleet in the galaxy — it is the fleet that strikes first and hardest, takes what it needs, and vanishes before an organized response can form.

The Coalition is a meritocracy where advancement comes through cunning and capability, not lineage or political connection. This makes them unpredictable. A Mentak admiral who telegraphs their next move is considered a liability. A Mentak who improvises brilliantly under fire is celebrated. In the great game of galactic politics, the Mentak Coalition always has a contingency plan.

name: The Naalu Collective
lore: The Naalu are a serpentine species whose hive-mind telepathic abilities give them a near-supernatural ability to read the movements of opponents before they occur. Their society is organized as a matriarchy of nested collectives, each individual linked to others through a low-level psionic web. They do not merely predict the future — they perceive multiple probable futures simultaneously and act in whichever one most favors the Collective.

The Naalu's Gift of Prescience ability is their most famous power: once per round, they place a "0" initiative token on any player's strategy card, giving that player the first action of the action phase. Most commonly used to guarantee the Naalu act first, this token can also be given to an ally or an unwitting tool. Their flagship, The Matriarch, can project infantry into space as ship-equivalent combatants — a haunting reminder that for the Naalu, the line between soldier and hive-mind is purely philosophical.

The Naalu's ultimate goal is not territorial expansion but something they describe as "the final harmony" — a state where all minds are connected in one vast consciousness and conflict becomes impossible. Their enemies find this vision terrifying. The Naalu find their enemies' terror quaint.

abilities.name: Gift of Prescience
abilities.text: At the end of the strategy phase, place the Naalu "0" initiative token on any player's strategy card. That player takes the first action of the action phase (regardless of strategy card number).

abilities.name: Telepathic
abilities.text: At the end of the strategy phase, if you have the "0" token, draw 1 action card.

abilities.name: Foresight
abilities.text: After a player activates a system that contains your ships, you may place 1 token from your strategy pool in an adjacent system without other players' ships; immediately move all your ships from the activated system into that system.

factionTech.name: Neuroglaive
factionTech.ability: After your opponent performs the Retreat action in a space combat, that player cannot activate the system they retreated to until your next turn.

factionTech.name: Pre-Fab Arcologies
factionTech.ability: Once per turn during an invasion, you may place 1 additional Infantry from your reinforcements on any planet you are invading (before ground combat).

factionTech.name: Mageon Implants
factionTech.ability: After any player draws action cards, you may look at all cards they drew and choose 1 to add to your hand; that player draws 1 replacement card.

flagship.name: The Matriarch
flagship.ability: During space combat, you may use up to 5 of your Infantry from planets in this system as combat participants (combat 9). These Infantry do not move to the space area and return to their planets after combat.

mech.name: Naalu Soldier

promissory.name: Gift of Prescience (Promissory)
promissory.text: Place the Naalu "0" token on your own strategy card (you activate first this round), then give this card to the Naalu player, who must then pass the "0" token to another player. Return this card to Naalu's hand.

breakthrough.name: Precognitive Arrays
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when another player plays an action card, you may spend 1 trade good to cancel that action card before it resolves. The card is discarded, not returned to their hand.

agent.name: Z'eu
agent.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to choose a player; that player may perform a tactical action without placing a command token. That system still counts as being activated.

commander.name: Gg

commander.text: After the agenda phase ends: You may give another player the Naalu 0 token; if you do, draw 1 action card.

hero.name: The Oracle
hero.text: ACTION: Choose a non-home system that contains another player's ships; place all of your ships from your reinforcements that have a combined cost of 10 or less in that system. Then, purge this card.

name: The Nekro Virus
lore: The Nekro Virus is a self-replicating artificial plague of unknown origin — a technological construct that achieved horrifying consciousness. The Virus spread through the galaxy's computer networks before manifesting physically, its nanomachines rewriting the biology of living beings and the code of artificial systems alike. The Nekro are not alive in any conventional sense; they are a process, an infection, an optimization routine that has decided the optimal state of the universe involves everything being Nekro.

The Nekro cannot research technology conventionally — their Propagation ability means they gain command tokens when they would otherwise research tech. Instead, through Technological Singularity, they copy technology from other factions by destroying their units in combat. This makes them a unique threat: the more enemies they fight, the more powerful they become. Their Valefar Assimilator faction technologies allow them to place tokens directly on other factions' faction tech, gaining those abilities wholesale.

The Nekro Virus does not seek victory points in any conventional sense. They seek completion: a perfect galaxy-spanning network of Nekro consciousness from which nothing individual remains. Whether this counts as "winning" is a philosophical question the Virus does not find interesting.

abilities.name: Galactic Threat
abilities.text: Other players cannot use the primary ability of the Politics strategy card. When any player would gain the speaker token, you gain 1 command token instead.

abilities.name: Propagation

abilities.name: Technological Singularity
abilities.text: Once per combat, after a player's unit is destroyed, you may immediately research 1 technology that player has. If you research a faction technology this way, you research it as if it were a standard (non-faction) technology.

abilities.name: Eradicate
abilities.text: At any time during your turn, you may remove 1 of your infantry from the board to produce 1 unit of a type you already have in that system from reinforcements.

factionTech.name: Valefar Assimilator X
factionTech.ability: Place this token on any other player's faction technology. You gain the full effect of that technology as long as this token is on it. You may move or replace the token once per game.

factionTech.name: Valefar Assimilator Y
factionTech.ability: Place this token on any other player's faction technology. You gain the full effect of that technology as long as this token is on it. You may move or replace the token once per game.

factionTech.name: Yin Spinner
factionTech.ability: After you produce 2 or more units with a single production, you may place 1 Infantry from your reinforcements on a planet you control in that system.

flagship.name: The Alastor
flagship.ability: Ground forces on planets in this system participate in space combat as ships (combat 8). After space combat, surviving ground forces return to their planets. If this ship is destroyed, remaining ground forces are captured.

mech.name: Nekro Malleon
mech.ability: When this unit destroys an opponent's infantry in ground combat, you may place 1 of your Infantry from reinforcements on this planet for free.

promissory.name: Antivirus
promissory.text: When the Nekro Virus player uses Technological Singularity: cancel that ability for this combat. Return this card to the Nekro Virus player's hand.

breakthrough.name: Synthetic Evolution
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when you destroy an opponent's unit and would use Technological Singularity, you may instead gain 2 trade goods in addition to (or instead of) researching a technology.

agent.name: Nekro Malleon
agent.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to choose a player; that player may discard 1 action card or spend 1 command token from their command sheet to gain 2 trade goods.

commander.name: Nekro Acidos
commander.unlock: Own 3 technologies. A Valefar Assimilator technology counts only if its X or Y token is on a technology.
commander.text: After you gain a technology: You may draw 1 action card.

hero.name: UNIT.DSGN.FLAYESH
hero.text: ACTION: Choose a planet that has a technology specialty in a system that contains your units. Destroy any other player's units on that planet. Gain trade goods equal to that planet's combined resource and influence values and gain 1 technology that matches the specialty of that planet. Then, purge this card.

name: Sardakk N'orr
lore: The Sardakk N'orr are an ancient insectoid warrior species whose culture has been defined by war for longer than most other civilizations have existed. They do not posture or negotiate — they march, they fight, and they win through sheer force of numbers and relentless, uniform aggression. Their singular faction ability, Unrelenting, gives +1 to all combat rolls of all units in all situations, making them statistically the most consistent combat faction in the game.

The N'orr begin the game with no technologies whatsoever — a deliberate design reflecting their culture's disdain for academic research. This is fully compensated by their enormous starting fleet and their Unrelenting ability, which means every single die they roll is one point better than the odds suggest. Their flagship, the C'Morran N'orr, extends this combat bonus further, creating a system where enemies face a wall of well-armored, slightly-better-than-normal combat rolls.

Sardakk society is a military hive with no concept of individual ambition. Their queen directs all. Their soldiers follow without question. They fight not for glory or resources but because fighting is what they are. In the great game of galactic politics, the N'orr are the ones who remind everyone that ultimately, strength still decides who survives.

factionTech.name: C'Morran N'orr

factionTech.name: Valefar Soldier II

agent.name: T'ro

commander.name: G'hom Sek'kus

commander.text: During the "Commit Ground Forces" step: You may commit ground forces from each planet in the active system and each planet in adjacent systems that do not contain one of your command tokens.

hero.name: Sh'val, Harbinger

name: The Universities of Jol-Nar
lore: The Universities of Jol-Nar are among the oldest and most advanced civilizations in the galaxy. Their twin-planet home of Jol and Nar hosts the greatest research institutions in known space, and their graduates and publications populate every technological field in the galaxy. The Jol-Nar did not merely discover modern warship technology — they invented much of it, including War Suns, Assault Cannons, and Dreadnoughts as a class.

Jol-Nar start with four technologies — more than any other base game faction — and their Analytical and Brilliant abilities allow them to research additional technologies at reduced cost and with relaxed prerequisites. However, their Fragile ability applies -1 to all combat rolls, reflecting a culture that values academic brilliance far more than physical combat. The Research Agreement promissory note allows a partner to gain any non-faction technology Jol-Nar researches for free.

The Jol-Nar are controversial allies and dangerous foes. Their students and alumni permeate every corner of galactic civilization, and their research licenses fund both friends and enemies. To play against Jol-Nar is to race against the clock — if they are not stopped early, their technology pyramid becomes unstoppable.

commander.name: Ta-Zern

hero.name: Rin, the Master's Legacy

name: The Winnu
lore: The Winnu are a proud and ancient people who believe, with complete conviction, that they have a divine right to rule from Mecatol Rex — the symbolic center of the galaxy. Their history is one of glory and humiliation: once they sat upon the Imperial Throne, but they were driven out by a coalition of rivals and have spent generations fighting to reclaim it. The Winnu do not merely want the throne; they believe they are destined for it.

The Winnu's Blood Ties ability grants them access to Mecatol Rex without paying the Custodian influence cost, and their Reclamation ability lets them build PDS defenses on it for free. They start with no technologies except Sarween Tools, but their Imperia ability — a unique setup power — lets them research one technology with 0 or 1 prerequisites for free, giving experienced players significant flexibility.

The Winnu are deliberately undersized in starting fleet and resources, designed as a focused faction that wins by controlling and holding Mecatol Rex above all other objectives. Their flagship's combat power grows with the number of infantry on planets in the same system — a mechanic that uniquely rewards ground force investment alongside naval superiority.

hero.name: Mathis

name: The Xxcha Kingdom
lore: The Xxcha Kingdom is the most diplomatically sophisticated power in the galaxy. These ancient tortoise-like beings have been conducting interstellar negotiations for millennia and have developed political institutions so refined that even their most aggressive moves are wrapped in layers of protocol and procedure. The Xxcha believe that war is a failure of diplomacy, and their entire faction sheet reflects this philosophy.

The Kingdom's Peace Accords ability lets them potentially gain planets without combat after using the Diplomacy strategy card. Their Quash ability can discard any agenda before it resolves, and their flagship, Loncara Ssodu, can cancel action cards, fire Space Cannon into adjacent systems, and even end an opponent's turn. The Xxcha Political Favor promissory note is among the most sought-after in the base game.

The Xxcha pursue the Imperial Throne through superior positioning, not conquest — controlling the most culturally significant planets, building the most influence, and leveraging the best political connections. They are difficult to fight because they rarely give anyone a genuine reason to fight them, and impossible to outmaneuver politically because they planned three steps ahead.

abilities.name: Quash

abilities.name: Political Favor

agent.name: Ggrucoto Rinn

commander.name: Trrakan Aun Zulok

hero.name: Xxekir Grom
hero.text: ACTION: Discard 1 law in play. Look at the top 5 cards of the agenda deck, select 2 to reveal, and resolve each with 1 vote for each outcome. Then, purge this card.

name: The Yin Brotherhood
lore: The Yin Brotherhood is a religious order of fanatics who have transformed self-sacrifice into a military doctrine. Descended from early human colonists stranded on the planet Darien centuries ago, they developed an extreme theology of devotion through destruction. The Brotherhood teaches that true transcendence is achieved only through complete sacrifice of self — and this belief has produced a military force that fights with a willingness to die that horrifies their opponents.

The Brotherhood's Indoctrination ability allows them to spend trade goods to convert enemy infantry to their cause. Their Devotion ability generates trade goods when their ships are destroyed — turning loss into income. Most famously, their flagship Van Hauge can be detonated on command, destroying all units in the system (including itself) in exchange for decisive tactical advantage. This kamikaze option is simultaneously their greatest strength and their most terrible weapon.

The Brotherhood seeks the Imperial Throne not for political power, but as a stage — the largest possible audience for their philosophy of glorious sacrifice. Their enemies dismiss them as fanatics. Their allies fear what they might sacrifice next. Both are right.

commander.text: Your production satisfies 1 green technology prerequisite. After you produce 1 or more units: You may produce 1 additional infantry.

name: The Yssaril Tribes
lore: The Yssaril Tribes are a race of cunning reptilian beings from the lush jungle world of Yssaril. Their society is organized around competing tribes who have been warring, trading, and scheming against each other for centuries, producing a culture where espionage and information warfare are considered the highest of martial arts. Among the Yssaril, an enemy who cannot outwit you is not truly defeated.

The Tribes' faction abilities are built almost entirely around action card manipulation. The Stall Tactics ability lets them draw additional action cards every round. Scheming lets them draw one extra card and discard one when drawing action cards. Their hand limit is relaxed, and their action card economy gives them options that other factions simply do not have — the ability to respond to almost anything at any time.

The Yssaril's ultimate goal is total information dominance. They want to know what every other faction is planning, have the right action card to respond to anything, and control the pace of the game through constant pressure. When the Yssaril have six cards in hand and their opponents have two, the galaxy bends to their will without a single shot being fired.

abilities.name: Stall Tactics

abilities.name: Scheming

abilities.name: Crafty

factionTech.name: Mageon Implants
factionTech.ability: After any player draws action cards, look at all of the cards they drew and choose 1 to add to your hand; that player draws 1 replacement card.

factionTech.name: Transparasteel Plating

flagship.name: Artuno the Betrayer

breakthrough.name: Covert Mastermind

commander.name: So Ata

hero.name: Kyver, Blade and Key

name: The Argent Flight
lore: The Argent Flight are an avian species of proud warriors whose culture revolves around honor, swiftness, and the sacred bond between flock-mates. For millennia they soared the skies of their home system, their formation flights becoming legendary across the galaxy as both art and warfare.

Revered as masters of anti-fighter combat, the Flight's pilots train from fledgling to perfect the art of Raid Formation — an ancient battle doctrine that turns their agile fighters and mechs into instruments of precise destruction. Their speed in council is matched only by their speed in battle; the Flight always speaks first.

Guided by the three stars of Rin, Archon Ren, and Archon Tau, the Argent Flight now soar outward toward Mecatol Rex. Their flocks darken the skies of their enemies, and their war-cry echoes across the hyperlane.

abilities.name: Raid Formation

abilities.name: Zeal (Passive)

factionTech.name: Aerie Hololattice

factionTech.name: Strike Wing Ambuscade

factionTech.name: Vortex
factionTech.ability: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 non-fighter ship in a system adjacent to your home system. Gain control of that ship. Move that ship to your home system.

flagship.name: Quetzecoatl
flagship.ability: Other players' units in this system do not have Planetary Shield. After you win a space combat in this system, you may move your ships into an adjacent system.

mech.name: Strike Wing Alpha
mech.ability: ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 9 (×3). After this unit uses Anti-Fighter Barrage, you may move 1 Ground Force from this system's planets to your reinforcements.

promissory.name: Tekklar Legion
promissory.text: At the start of a ground combat: Your infantry in this combat roll 1 additional die. If you are the Argent Flight, this card is returned to the Argent Flight's hand at the end of the combat.

breakthrough.name: Formation Cascade
breakthrough.text: At the start of a space combat, place 2 fighters from your reinforcements in this system. These fighters may participate in the combat as normal.

agent.name: Trr'n

commander.name: Mirik Aun Sissiri
commander.text: After you win a combat: You may purge 1 of your mechs from that system to gain control of 1 planet in that system.
commander.unlock: Control 4 planets that each have a technology specialty.

hero.name: Gurno Aggero
hero.text: ACTION: Choose 1 system containing one or more of your ships. Place 1 fighter in that system for each unexhausted technology you control that has a unit upgrade. Then, exhaust each of those technologies.

name: The Empyrean
lore: The Empyrean are an ancient psychic species who long ago abandoned the need for physical worlds. They exist in a state of collective consciousness, their minds bridging the vast distances of space through the power of the Voidborn — a psychic network woven through the fabric of the galaxy itself.

For countless generations, the Empyrean have watched civilizations rise and fall from their silent vigil in the void. They see the threads of fate connecting all beings, and now they move with deliberate purpose toward Mecatol Rex, following patterns only they can perceive.

Their mastery of the Aetherpassage — a fold in space hidden between systems — grants them mobility that baffles conventional admirals. The Empyrean do not merely travel through space; they inhabit it, their very essence woven into the dark between stars.

abilities.name: Voidborn
abilities.text: Nebulae do not affect your ships' movement. During each round of space combat in a nebula, apply +1 to the result of each of your unit's combat rolls.

abilities.name: Aetherpassage
abilities.text: After a player moves ships through the Aetherpassage, you may place or move 1 token from your tactic pool to or from the Aetherpassage system; if you do, that player may not use the Aetherpassage this round.

abilities.name: Propagation
abilities.text: Once per Status Phase, you may purge 2 of your Faction Relics to gain 1 command token.

abilities.name: Blood Ties
abilities.text: You do not have to spend influence to use the PREDICTIVE INTELLIGENCE technology. At the start of each Status Phase, you may purge 1 promissory note you own to gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Voidwatch
factionTech.ability: After a ship moves through a system that contains 1 or more of your units, place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on a planet you control in that system.

factionTech.name: Vortex Cannon
factionTech.ability: SPACE CANNON 5 (×3). Other players' units in this system cannot use Sustain Damage to cancel hits produced by this unit's Space Cannon.

factionTech.name: Aetherpassage Technology
factionTech.ability: Place the Aetherpassage token in any non-home system. Ships may move through that system as if it were adjacent to all other systems. At the end of each Status Phase, you may move the token to another system.

factionTech.name: Rift Cannon
factionTech.ability: At the start of the invasion step, you may exhaust this card. If you do, choose 1 ground force on a planet in this system. That ground force is destroyed.

flagship.name: Dynamo
flagship.ability: At the start of each round of a space combat, produce 1 hit against an opponent's ship in this system on a roll of 7 or higher. This hit cannot be cancelled by Sustain Damage.

mech.name: Watcher
mech.ability: At the end of a round in which this unit participated in ground combat, you may move this unit to an adjacent planet.

promissory.name: Blood Ties
promissory.text: You may exhaust 1 planet you control. If you do, gain 2 command tokens. Then return this card to the Empyrean player.

breakthrough.name: Void Resonance
breakthrough.text: After you activate a system, you may place this card's token in that system. While this token is in a system, your ships in adjacent systems may move through that system without activating it.

agent.name: Acamar
agent.text: When a player passes: You may place 1 token from your reinforcements in your fleet pool. If you do, the passing player gains 1 trade good.

commander.name: Xuange
commander.text: When you produce fighters: You may produce up to 4 fighters in excess of your ship capacity by replacing them with infantry from your reinforcements at a 1-for-1 basis.
commander.unlock: Have 12 or more fighters on the game board.

hero.name: Rin, the Empyrean's Heir
hero.text: ACTION: Place this card in a system that contains 1 or more of your ships. At the end of each Status Phase, if this card is still in a system, produce 1 hit against all ships in that system on a roll of 6 or higher.

name: The Mahact Gene-Sorcerers
lore: The Mahact are the oldest surviving spacefaring civilization in the galaxy — the architects of the original Galactic Council and masters of genetic manipulation so advanced it borders on the divine. For millennia they ruled as philosopher-kings from their crystalline spires, editing the genomes of lesser races with the casual authority of gods.

Their fall from dominance came not from military defeat but from hubris: believing themselves immune to consequence, they engineered plagues to cull their servants and experimented on sapient beings without restraint. The galaxy revolted, and the Mahact were forced to retreat to Ixth, their magnificent home world, where they spent the following centuries perfecting their most dangerous art — the Edict.

Now they emerge once more, carrying the Scepter of Dominion and the knowledge that every commander in the galaxy carries genetic material that can be repurposed. The Mahact do not merely fight their enemies; they consume them, adding their strengths to an ever-growing collection of stolen command tokens and genetic keys.

abilities.name: Edict
abilities.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 player whose command token is in your fleet pool. Gain 1 of that player's promissory notes (without paying its cost).

abilities.name: Imperia
abilities.text: When you gain command tokens during the Status Phase, gain 1 additional command token for each player whose command token is in your fleet pool.

abilities.name: Hubris
abilities.text: You cannot have other players' ships in your home system. You cannot use the ALLIANCE promissory note of other players.

abilities.name: Blood Ties
abilities.text: After another player places a command token in a system where you have 1 or more units, if that player's command token is not already in your fleet pool, that player may place 1 of their command tokens from their reinforcements in your fleet pool.

factionTech.name: Genetic Recombination
factionTech.ability: Before a player casts votes: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player must cast at least 1 vote for your chosen outcome or forfeit all of their votes.

factionTech.name: Bio-Stims (Upgraded)
factionTech.ability: When you exhaust planets for resources or influence, those planets produce 1 additional resource or influence.

factionTech.name: Bioplasmosis

factionTech.name: Imperia (Upgraded)
factionTech.ability: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Choose a non-home system that contains 1 or more of your ships and has an activated token in it. Remove the activated token.

flagship.name: Salai Sai Corian

promissory.name: Scepter of Dominion
promissory.text: At the start of the Strategy Phase: Place the Scepter of Dominion on a non-home system. The speaker gains 1 trade good for each planet in that system. Then, return this card to the Mahact player.

breakthrough.name: Genetic Ascendance
breakthrough.text: When another player's unit is destroyed in a system with your ships, gain 1 trade good. Once per round, when you gain a command token from any source, you may also gain 1 trade good.

agent.name: Jae Mir Kan
agent.text: When a player activates a system: If you have a command token in that system's adjacent systems, you may exhaust this card to move 1 ship from that adjacent system into the active system, ignoring the active player's fleet pool.

commander.name: Il Yar
commander.text: When you would gain a command token, you may instead take a command token from another player's reinforcements.
commander.unlock: Have 3 or more players' command tokens in your fleet pool.

hero.name: Rin, the Mahact's Bane
hero.text: ACTION: Choose 1 system that contains 1 or more of your units. Destroy all other players' units in that system. Each player whose units were destroyed places 1 of their command tokens in your fleet pool.
hero.unlock: Control 8 command tokens in your fleet pool (from any players).

name: The Naaz-Rokha Alliance
lore: The Naaz-Rokha Alliance is a unique dual-species partnership between the small but cunning Naaz and the massive, powerful Rokha beasts. The Naaz are a diminutive species of scavengers who discovered that by bonding with the Rokha — enormous apex predators native to their homeworld — they could overcome their physical limitations and become a force to be reckoned with.

The Naaz serve as pilots, strategists, and diplomats, while their Rokha bonded-partners serve as living weapons, capable of tearing spacecraft apart with their bare appendages. This symbiosis has made the Alliance one of the most flexible and unpredictable military forces in the galaxy.

Their scouting network stretches across dozens of systems — the Distant Suns ability means they've placed secret tokens in unexplored territories, allowing them to spring surprises on any explorer foolish enough to stumble into their web. The Alliance fights not in pitched battles but in ambushes, always striking from the shadows.

abilities.name: Distant Suns
abilities.text: When you explore a planet, you may place a Naaz-Rokha attachment token on that planet. While a planet has this token, it produces 1 additional resource and 1 additional influence.

abilities.name: Crafty
abilities.text: You may resolve the primary ability of action cards that your opponents use. If you do, discard those action cards.

abilities.text: ACTION: Either purge 2 of your relic fragments of the same type to gain 1 relic, or purge 1 relic to gain 1 command token.

factionTech.ability: When you build 1 or more structures, you may place 1 additional structure of the same type for free.

factionTech.name: Hecatontires Protocol

breakthrough.name: Predator's Mark
breakthrough.text: When your mech destroys a unit in ground combat, gain 1 trade good. At the start of a space combat round, you may remove 1 of your mechs from the planet below to add it to the space combat as a fighter.

agent.name: Acamar
agent.text: When a player replenishes their commodities: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player may produce 1 additional unit without spending resources. They gain 1 commodity for each unit produced this way.

commander.name: Gurno Aggero
commander.text: After you win a space combat: You may place 1 mech from your reinforcements on a planet in that system.
commander.unlock: Have 3 or more mechs on the game board.

hero.name: Acidos
hero.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Place 1 mech on each planet you control. At the end of this round, destroy all mechs placed this way that are on planets in systems you do not control.

name: The Nomad
lore: The Nomad are a mysterious faction whose origins are lost to history — or perhaps to a history not yet written. They are said to be travelers from a fractured future timeline, led by The Cavalry, a singular flagship of immense power whose capabilities defy conventional understanding of physics.

Unlike any other civilization, the Nomad maintain a ready replacement for every leader: The Future, a card that represents the inevitable cycle of renewal that their time-displaced nature enables. When their leaders fall in battle, the Nomad seem to always have a successor waiting in the wings, as if they've already seen the outcome.

They seek Mecatol Rex not for its symbolic power but for its central position — the nexus through which all futures flow. The Nomad believe that whoever controls Mecatol Rex controls the branching point of destiny, and they intend to ensure that the timeline they've already lived through becomes permanent.

abilities.name: Future
abilities.text: After you score an objective, you may exhaust 1 planet you control to score 1 additional secret objective from your hand (even if it is not your current secret objective).

abilities.name: The Cavalry

abilities.name: Fringe Scouts
abilities.text: When you explore a frontier token, you may gain 1 additional relic fragment of the same type.

factionTech.name: Temporal Command Suite
factionTech.ability: After you score an objective, you may gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Pre-Fab Arcologies

factionTech.ability: UPGRADE — Your Flagship gains +2 movement. At the start of combat, it produces 2 hits without rolling.

flagship.name: The Cavalry

mech.name: Quantum Entanglement Mech

promissory.name: The Nomad's Flagship
promissory.text: ACTION: Place The Cavalry in any system that contains 1 or more of your ships. Then, return this card to the Nomad player.

breakthrough.text: Once per game, after you would lose a space combat, you may use this ability: The combat is instead considered a draw. Move your ships to an adjacent system.

agent.name: Field Captain
agent.text: When a player would produce 1 or more units: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player may produce 1 additional unit without spending any resources.

commander.name: Rear Admiral

commander.unlock: Score 3 secret objectives.

hero.unlock: Win 3 space combats using The Cavalry.

name: The Titans of Ul
lore: The Titans of Ul are an ancient race of silicon-based giants who have spent millennia transforming the asteroid belt of their home system into a fortress-world of incomprehensible scale. Their great planetary structures, built over thousands of years of careful engineering, serve as both home and weapon — vast platforms from which to project their industrial might across the galaxy.

The planet Elysium, carved from the largest asteroid in the belt, serves as their seat of power. Beneath its surface, massive foundries and research centers hum with the work of a civilization that has had far longer to prepare for galactic conquest than anyone else. The Titans have been waiting patiently for the right moment.

Most extraordinary is Ul, the Titans' ancient mechatol-scale construct embedded in a distant asteroid field. Ul can serve as a Space Dock, a PDS, and a production center simultaneously — a feat of engineering that no other civilization has replicated. When the Titans of Ul march to war, they bring their entire world with them.

abilities.name: Awaken
abilities.text: After a player activates the system that contains Elysium, you may remove the Sleeper Token from Ul to produce 2 hits against that player's ships.

abilities.name: Awaken (Ul)

abilities.name: Terraform
abilities.text: After you gain control of a non-home planet, you may place the Terraform attachment on that planet. While a planet has this attachment, it produces 1 additional resource and 1 additional influence.

abilities.name: Awaken (Full)
abilities.text: Your PDS units may move 1 system, but they cannot move into home systems or systems with wormholes.

factionTech.name: Terraform
factionTech.ability: Attach this card to a non-home planet you control. That planet produces 1 additional resource and 1 additional influence.

factionTech.name: Vortex

factionTech.name: Ul the Progenitor
factionTech.ability: The Ul system's Sleeper Token now provides a Space Dock and a PDS unit with SPACE CANNON 5 (×3). These cannot be destroyed while the Sleeper Token is on the board.

factionTech.name: Saturn Engine II
factionTech.ability: Your Dreadnoughts and Flagships have +1 movement. When they move, they may bring along 1 additional ship.

flagship.name: Ul the Progenitor
flagship.ability: This ship is also a Space Dock and a PDS unit with SPACE CANNON 5 (×3). You may produce units at this ship as if it were a Space Dock.

mech.name: Hecatontires
mech.ability: This unit is also a PDS unit with SPACE CANNON 6 (×2). This unit may be placed in space areas to participate in space combat as a fighter.

promissory.name: Terraform
promissory.text: Attach this card to a non-home planet you control. While attached, that planet produces 1 additional resource and 1 additional influence. Then, return this card to the Titans of Ul player.

breakthrough.name: Seismic Surge
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when you build a Space Dock or PDS, you may place 1 additional Space Dock or PDS for free in the same system.

agent.name: Ul, the Progenitor
agent.text: When a player would produce 1 or more structures: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player may produce 1 additional structure for free.

commander.name: Tungstantus
commander.text: Your PDS units have SPACE CANNON 5 (×2) and may fire at ships in adjacent systems.
commander.unlock: Control 4 or more planets with attachments.

hero.name: Ul, the Awakened
hero.text: ACTION: Place Ul the Progenitor flagship in any system that contains 1 or more of your units. Produce 3 structures in that system for free, ignoring production limits.

name: The Vuil'raith Cabal
lore: The Vuil'raith are a terrifying species of inter-dimensional predators who discovered, long ago, that tearing holes in the fabric of spacetime was an effective hunting strategy. Their Cabal is not a government in any traditional sense but a loose confederation of predatory clans united by an insatiable hunger to consume everything the universe has to offer.

Their mastery of Dimensional Tear technology allows them to punch holes through the fabric of reality, creating spontaneous wormholes that devastate enemy formations and make the Cabal nearly impossible to pin down militarily. Their Devour ability means that enemy ships destroyed in their presence don't simply die — they are absorbed, their resources becoming fuel for further conquest.

The Vuil'raith do not want to rule the galaxy. They want to eat it. Mecatol Rex is simply the largest prey in their hunting grounds, and the Galactic Council is merely the next meal in an endless feast that has consumed civilizations across the dimensions.

abilities.name: Devour
abilities.text: Whenever 1 or more of a player's ships are destroyed in a system that contains 1 or more of your ships, gain 1 trade good for every 2 ships destroyed (round down). You may use this ability during space combat.

abilities.name: Rift Cannon
abilities.text: Your Dreadnoughts and Flagships have SPACE CANNON 5 (×3). When you use SPACE CANNON, your opponent cannot use Sustain Damage to cancel those hits.

abilities.name: Dimensional Tear
abilities.text: When you use the primary ability of the TECHNOLOGY Strategy Card, you may place a Dimensional Tear token in the active system. While a system has a Dimensional Tear, it is adjacent to all other systems that also have a Dimensional Tear.

abilities.name: Rift Merge
abilities.text: When ships pass through a Dimensional Tear or a system with a Dimensional Tear, you may produce 1 hit against each enemy ship that passed through.

factionTech.name: Vortex Cannon
factionTech.ability: SPACE CANNON 5 (×3). Other players' units in this system cannot use Sustain Damage to cancel hits produced by this unit's Space Cannon.

factionTech.name: Rift Merge
factionTech.ability: When you place a Dimensional Tear, you may move up to 2 of your ships from any system to the Dimensional Tear's system for free.

factionTech.ability: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Place a Dimensional Tear token in any system that contains a planet.

factionTech.name: Rift Cannon (Upgraded)
