flagship.name: C'morran N'orr
flagship.ability: When this ship produces hits during combat, each player whose ships are destroyed may not use Sustain Damage. After this ship produces hits, gain 1 trade good for each ship destroyed this way.

mech.name: Dimension Ripper
mech.ability: At the start of a round of ground combat, you may produce 2 hits automatically. If you do, this unit cannot roll dice in this round of combat.

promissory.name: Tekklar Legion
promissory.text: At the start of a ground combat: Your infantry in this combat roll 1 additional die. If the Vuil'raith player is the active player in this combat, this card is returned to the Vuil'raith player at the end of the combat.

breakthrough.name: Void Feeder
breakthrough.text: When a Dimensional Tear is placed in a system that contains ships, produce 1 hit against each enemy ship in that system. Your ships in systems with Dimensional Tears gain +1 to all combat rolls.

agent.name: Acidos
agent.text: When a player activates a system: If that system has a Dimensional Tear, you may exhaust this card. If you do, place 1 Dimensional Tear in an adjacent system.

commander.name: Ggrucoto Ransen
commander.text: After your ships destroy an enemy ship in a system, gain 1 commodity. At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each Dimensional Tear on the board.
commander.unlock: Destroy 15 ships total across all combats.

hero.name: It Feeds on Carrion
hero.text: ACTION: Choose up to 3 systems that have Dimensional Tears. Destroy all ships (friend and foe) in those systems. For each ship destroyed, gain 1 command token.
hero.unlock: Have 4 or more Dimensional Tears on the game board.

name: The Council Keleres
lore: The Council Keleres is not a faction born of a single species but a coalition of three member groups drawn from existing galactic powers: the Mentak Coalition, the Xxcha Kingdom, and the Argent Flight. United under the banner of Galactic law and galactic oversight, the Keleres operate from the corridors of Mecatol Rex itself, wielding the authority of the Galactic Council as both shield and sword.

The founding of the Keleres was a controversial act. Critics argue that the Council has granted a faction unprecedented access to the galaxy's legal and military infrastructure. Supporters counter that the chaos following the last imperial era demanded exactly this kind of decisive, centralized governance. The truth, as always, lies somewhere in the politics.

Players choosing the Council Keleres must select one of three subfaction identities at game start: the Mentak Keleres, the Xxcha Keleres, or the Argent Keleres. This choice determines their home system, starting units, and a portion of their starting technologies and abilities — but all three share the unique Custodia Vigilia mechanic and the power of the Galactic Council itself.

abilities.name: Custodia Vigilia
abilities.text: After you gain control of Mecatol Rex, place the Custodia Vigilia token on that planet. While Mecatol Rex has this token, you may exhaust it to cast 1 additional vote during the Agenda Phase and to use the Galactic Council's abilities as if you were the speaker.

abilities.name: Scepter of Dominion (Keleres)
abilities.text: After you score an objective, you may gain 1 command token and place 1 fighter from your reinforcements in any system that contains 1 or more of your ships.

abilities.name: Sufferance
abilities.text: Agendas you vote against can still pass. When an agenda passes, you may exhaust 1 planet you control to gain 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Galactic Mandate
abilities.text: After each Agenda Phase, gain 1 trade good for each agenda that passed that benefited you or your allies. You may spend these trade goods as influence.

abilities.name: Mentak Subfaction Ability
abilities.text: (Mentak Keleres only) Once per round, when another player spends trade goods, you may gain 1 trade good. Your flagship gains SPACE CANNON 6 (×3).

abilities.name: Xxcha Subfaction Ability
abilities.text: (Xxcha Keleres only) After you cast votes on an agenda, you may cast 1 additional vote for each player who voted the same way as you.

abilities.name: Argent Subfaction Ability
abilities.text: (Argent Keleres only) You always vote during the Agenda Phase before any other player. You may cast 1 additional vote for each unique technology color you control.

startingTech: (1 additional tech from your subfaction's starting tech)

factionTech.name: Iiharan Array
factionTech.ability: At the start of the Agenda Phase, you may look at the top 3 Agenda cards. Place them back in any order.

factionTech.name: Rin's Assimilator X
factionTech.ability: When another player scores an objective, you may place a score marker on that same objective, gaining the same victory points, if you also meet the requirements.

factionTech.name: Rin's Assimilator Y
factionTech.ability: When you would gain a technology, instead copy a technology already owned by another player.

factionTech.name: Custodia Vigilia (Upgraded)
factionTech.ability: Your Custodia Vigilia token grants you +2 votes during the Agenda Phase instead of +1. You may exhaust it twice per Agenda Phase.

flagship.name: Ggrucoto
flagship.ability: When another player's ship is destroyed in this system, you may place that player's command token in your fleet pool. Command tokens placed this way are not removed during the Status Phase.

mech.name: Keleres Protector
mech.ability: After this unit participates in a ground combat, if it survives, gain 1 trade good.

promissory.name: Council's Vow
promissory.text: At the start of the Agenda Phase: Cast 3 additional votes on any agenda. Those votes can be used by any player of your choice. Then, return this card to the Council Keleres player.

breakthrough.name: Mandate of Heaven
breakthrough.text: After an agenda resolves, if the outcome you voted for passed, gain 1 command token and 1 trade good. Once per Agenda Phase, you may look at 1 agenda card from the discard pile.

agent.name: Suffi An
agent.text: When a player casts votes on an agenda: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player may recast their votes for a different outcome, gaining 1 trade good for switching.

commander.name: Xxcha Councilor
commander.text: After you pass an agenda you voted for: Gain 1 command token and draw 1 action card.
commander.unlock: Pass 3 agendas you voted for.

hero.name: The Keleres Champion
hero.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 agenda from the discard pile. Resolve that agenda as if it had just passed, applying its effects immediately.

name: The Crimson Rebellion
lore: The Crimson Rebellion arose from the ashes of a dozen oppressed civilizations that had suffered under the old imperial order. Once scattered servants, laborers, and slaves scattered across the outer rim, they found in their shared suffering a bond stronger than any empire — and in their stolen technology, the tools to fight back.

Their signature innovation is the weaponization of epsilon wormholes — unstable spatial tears that conventional factions consider too dangerous to exploit. The Crimson Rebellion's navigators have learned to breach these hazards, arriving where no enemy expects them and striking before defenses can be prepared. Their motto: "Strike where they cannot follow, hide where they cannot reach."

The Rebellion's social structure is built around cells rather than hierarchy. No single leader can be captured and turned, because every captain knows only their immediate mission and the location of one safe harbor. They do not seek an empire — they seek the end of all empires, including, ultimately, their own.

abilities.name: Sundered
abilities.text: Your ships may move through systems that have epsilon wormholes without suffering their effects. You may treat epsilon wormhole systems as adjacent when determining movement.

abilities.name: Breach
abilities.text: When you activate a system that contains an epsilon wormhole, you may place 1 Breach token in that system. While a system has a Breach token, all players may treat it as a wormhole system.

abilities.name: Cell Structure
abilities.text: When your ships are destroyed in combat, you may return up to 2 of your surviving ships to your reinforcements instead of leaving the system. Those ships may be redeployed normally.

abilities.name: Epsilon Drive
abilities.text: Once per round, when you move ships, you may move them an additional system if they move through or to a system that contains an epsilon wormhole.

factionTech.name: Epsilon Cascade
factionTech.ability: When you place a Breach token, also place 1 Breach token in an adjacent system. Ships moving through Breach systems may do so without activating them.

factionTech.name: Void Piercer
factionTech.ability: Your ships may move through systems containing enemy ships without engaging in combat, provided they began movement in a system with a Breach token.

factionTech.name: Rebellion Protocol
factionTech.ability: After another player scores an objective, you may draw 1 action card. If that player has more victory points than you, draw 2 action cards instead.

factionTech.name: Sundered Armament
factionTech.ability: Your Destroyers have ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 6 (×3) and may use Space Cannon 7 (×2). When your ships move through an epsilon wormhole, they may fire Space Cannon before combat begins.

flagship.name: The Crimson Tide
flagship.ability: This ship may move through systems containing enemy ships. After it moves, produce 2 hits against any ships in systems it passed through. Those ships cannot use Sustain Damage against these hits.

mech.name: Epsilon Striker
mech.ability: When this unit is placed on a planet, you may immediately move it to any other planet in a system that has a Breach token. BOMBARDMENT 7 (×2).

promissory.name: Revolutionary Compact
promissory.text: At the start of your turn: Move any number of your ships from systems with Breach tokens to any other systems with Breach tokens. Then, return this card to the Crimson Rebellion player.

breakthrough.name: Rebel's Gambit
breakthrough.text: Once per round, after you lose a space combat, you may place 1 Breach token in that system. Your retreating ships may move to any system with a Breach token instead of an adjacent system.

agent.name: Commander Vex
agent.text: When a player activates a system: If that system has a Breach token, you may exhaust this card. If you do, place 1 of your ships from your reinforcements in that system.

commander.name: Admiral Sunder
commander.text: Your Destroyers have +1 movement. When you activate a system, if you moved through 1 or more Breach tokens, you may place 1 additional Destroyer from your reinforcements in the activated system.
commander.unlock: Place 5 Breach tokens total across all systems.

hero.name: The Breach-Born
hero.text: ACTION: Remove all Breach tokens from the game board. For each Breach token removed, place 1 ship of your choice from your reinforcements in any system you control.
hero.unlock: Win 3 combats in systems with Breach tokens.

name: The Deepwrought Scholarate
lore: The Deepwrought Scholarate are a civilization of deep-sea beings who evolved in the crushing pressures of sub-oceanic trenches, developing extraordinary intellects in isolation from the rest of the galaxy. When they finally built vessels capable of reaching orbit, they approached spacefaring with the same patient, systematic methodology they applied to everything: observe, theorize, test, refine.

Their unique gift is the ability to reconfigure their technology in the field. Where other factions are locked into fixed research trees, the Scholarate can "deep-forge" existing technologies into hybrid variants, creating units and abilities that their opponents have never encountered before. Every battle against the Scholarate is a battle against an unknown weapon system.

The Scholarate does not value conquest for its own sake. They seek data — the kind that only comes from war. Their move toward Mecatol Rex is fundamentally a research expedition, and every faction they encounter is a specimen to be studied, catalogued, and if necessary, dissected.

abilities.name: Deep Forge
abilities.text: When you research a technology, you may instead "deep-forge" two technologies you already own: flip one face-down and gain one of its prerequisite-reduced benefits as a persistent passive effect.

abilities.name: Scholastic Method
abilities.text: Once per Status Phase, you may look at the top 3 technology cards in the deck. Place them back in any order. You may keep 1 of them in your hand.

abilities.name: Pressure Adaptation
abilities.text: Your ships and infantry are never affected by Gravity Rifts or Supernovas. When your units are in systems that would normally destroy them, they are simply unable to enter instead.

abilities.name: Iterative Refinement
abilities.text: When you research a technology that you already own a previous version of, gain 1 trade good and 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Abyssal Engine
factionTech.ability: Your Dreadnoughts and Flagships have +1 movement. When they move, they do not activate the systems they pass through.

factionTech.name: Deep Forge Mastery
factionTech.ability: You may deep-forge up to 2 technologies per Status Phase. Each deep-forged technology grants a different bonus of your choice: +1 movement to 1 unit type, or BOMBARDMENT 7 to 1 unit type, or SPACE CANNON 6 to 1 unit type.

factionTech.name: Pressure Weaponization
factionTech.ability: Your Dreadnoughts and Cruisers have BOMBARDMENT 5 (×2). When you bombard a planet, ignore its Planetary Shield.

factionTech.name: Scholastic Synthesis
factionTech.ability: Once per game, copy 1 faction technology from any player and add it to your technology tree for the rest of the game.

flagship.name: The Deepwrought
flagship.ability: At the start of each round of space combat, you may deep-forge 1 technology: choose 1 technology you control and give this ship either +2 combat, +1 movement, or SPACE CANNON 5 (×2) for this round.

mech.name: Pressure Unit
mech.ability: At the start of each round of ground combat, choose 1: this unit has +2 combat this round, or produces 1 automatic hit and then cannot roll dice.

promissory.name: Research Compact
promissory.text: When you research a technology: You may research 1 additional technology, ignoring prerequisites. Then, return this card to the Deepwrought Scholarate player.

breakthrough.name: Abyssal Breakthrough
breakthrough.text: Once per game, when you research a technology, you may instead copy the exact faction technology of any player (ignoring faction restrictions). Treat it as your own technology.

agent.name: Senior Scholar
agent.text: When a player researches a technology: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player may research 1 additional technology this action, reducing its prerequisites by 1 of any color.

commander.name: Arch-Scholar Nessik
commander.text: Once per round, when you deep-forge a technology, you may apply its benefit to all of your ships in any 1 system instead of just 1 unit type.
commander.unlock: Own 8 or more technologies.

hero.name: The Deep Mind
hero.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Look at all technology cards in every player's hand and all technology cards in the technology deck. You may take 1 technology card from any of these sources and add it to your hand.
hero.unlock: Research 5 technologies in a single game.

name: The Firmament
lore: The Firmament and The Obsidian are two aspects of a single, sundered consciousness — once a unified civilization of crystalline beings who shattered themselves across two separate forms to survive an ancient catastrophe. The Firmament are the visible face: orderly, diplomatic, and radiant with purpose. The Obsidian are the hidden face: ruthless, calculating, and capable of atrocities the Firmament publicly disavows.

Their unique "dual faction" mechanic reflects this split nature. Players command both aspects simultaneously, with a hand of Plot cards that can be played as either faction depending on what the moment demands. The Firmament scores objectives openly; The Obsidian scores through subterfuge and sabotage. Together, they are more dangerous than either alone.

Both factions share the dream of reunification — but they disagree violently on what that means. The Firmament believes they must first cleanse the galaxy of chaos, then merge in triumph. The Obsidian believes they must tear everything down and rebuild from darkness. Mecatol Rex is the prize they both seek, for opposite reasons.

abilities.name: Dual Nature
abilities.text: You have two faction sheets: The Firmament (light) and The Obsidian (shadow). At the start of each round, assign your ships to one of the two aspects. Each aspect has its own abilities and victory conditions.

abilities.name: Plot Cards
abilities.text: You have a hand of Plot cards that may be played as either faction. Plot cards can be used to score covert objectives (Obsidian) or to trigger powerful battlefield effects (Firmament).

abilities.name: Resonance (Firmament)
abilities.text: When you score an objective as The Firmament, gain 1 Plot card. (See also The Obsidian section for the Obsidian scoring effect of this ability.)

abilities.name: Fracture
abilities.text: Once per game, you may declare Fracture: split all your units evenly between your two aspects. Both aspects score independently for the rest of the game, and the total of both aspects' VP counts as your score.

factionTech.name: Resonance Array
factionTech.ability: When you play a Plot card, gain 1 trade good. You may hold up to 7 Plot cards in your hand instead of 5.

factionTech.name: Crystalline Lattice
factionTech.ability: When The Firmament scores a public objective, you may immediately score 1 secret objective from your hand without revealing it publicly.

factionTech.name: Total Resonance
factionTech.ability: Once per game, when you play Fracture, move all of your ships from any systems to your home system for free. Then, redistribute them between aspects as you choose.

flagship.name: The Shard
flagship.ability: This flagship may be assigned to either aspect freely. When it is in a system, all enemy ships in adjacent systems lose 1 from all combat rolls. After combat, gain 1 Plot card.

mech.name: Crystalline Warrior
mech.ability: When this unit participates in ground combat, you may play 1 Plot card to either double this unit's combat rolls this round or to immediately end the combat as a draw.

promissory.name: Resonant Oath
promissory.text: When you make a deal with another player: Both players gain 1 Plot card. If the deal is broken, the offending player loses 1 victory point. Then, return this card to The Firmament / The Obsidian player.

breakthrough.name: The Fracture Point
breakthrough.text: Once per game, when you activate Fracture, you may score 1 additional secret objective from your hand immediately without meeting its requirements.

agent.name: The Crystal Voice
agent.text: When a player votes on an agenda: You may exhaust this card. If you do, gain 1 Plot card and you may cast 1 additional vote on that agenda for free.

name: The Obsidian
lore: The Obsidian is the hidden face of the dual faction: ruthless, calculating, and capable of atrocities The Firmament publicly disavows. Where The Firmament wins points openly through diplomacy and control, The Obsidian scores through subterfuge, sabotage, and secret objectives. See The Firmament section above for shared faction lore, starting units, flagship, mech, promissory note, and breakthrough.

abilities.name: Resonance (Obsidian)
abilities.text: When you score an objective as The Obsidian, all other players lose 1 victory point if they cannot pay 1 trade good as tribute.

factionTech.name: Obsidian Protocol
factionTech.ability: The Obsidian aspect's ships have +1 combat when attacking from systems adjacent to systems controlled by The Firmament aspect.

commander.name: The Shadow Hand
commander.text: Once per round, when you play a Plot card as The Obsidian, you may play a second Plot card for free.
commander.unlock: Play 5 Plot cards total.

hero.name: The Resonant One
hero.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Score 1 secret objective from your hand without meeting its requirements. Then, choose 1 other player: they must also score 1 of their secret objectives, revealing it publicly.

name: Last Bastion
lore: Last Bastion is not the name of a species but a statement of purpose. They are the survivors — the last remnant of a once-great empire annihilated by a force whose name is no longer spoken aloud. Their home system is a fortress built from the wreckage of their fallen civilization, armed with every weapon their dying society could devise before the end.

The survivors who built Last Bastion did not emerge from hiding to seek revenge. They emerged because something followed them across the void, and they know that if they do not hold the line, there will be no galaxy left to squabble over. Their Galvanize ability reflects this: they are the last wall between the galaxy and oblivion, and they know it.

To other factions, Last Bastion appears reckless — they commit to every battle as though it might be the final one. In truth, that is exactly how they think. Every victory is borrowed time. Every alliance is a temporary measure. The only permanent victory is total annihilation of the threat, and Mecatol Rex is the command throne they need to coordinate the galaxy's final defense.

abilities.name: Galvanize
abilities.text: Once per round, when another player is attacked, you may move any number of your ships to that system without activating it. Your ships fight alongside that player for the duration of the combat.

abilities.name: Final Stand
abilities.text: When your ships are fighting in your home system, all your units gain +1 to all combat rolls and cannot be forced to retreat.

abilities.name: Fortify
abilities.text: At the end of each round, you may place 1 additional PDS or Ground Force on any planet you control for free.

abilities.name: Desperate Hour
abilities.text: When you take casualties in combat, each destroyed ship produces 1 final hit before being removed. This ability may only trigger once per ship.

factionTech.name: Bastion Wall
factionTech.ability: Your PDS units have SPACE CANNON 5 (×3) and may fire at ships 2 systems away instead of 1. In your home system, PDS units fire at 4 (×3).

factionTech.name: Fortification Protocol
factionTech.ability: When you build a Space Dock on a planet, also place 1 PDS on that planet for free. When your Space Dock is destroyed, it produces 3 hits before being removed.

factionTech.name: Galvanize Protocol
factionTech.ability: You may use Galvanize twice per round. When you Galvanize into a combat you win, gain 1 trade good for each of your ships that survived.

factionTech.name: Last Line
factionTech.ability: Once per game, when your home system would be captured, cancel that capture. All enemy units in your home system are destroyed. Then, place 3 infantry and 1 PDS on each of your home system's planets.

flagship.name: The Final Bulwark
flagship.ability: When another player's home system is threatened (contains enemy ships), you may immediately move this flagship to that system without activating it. While in another player's home system, this flagship has +2 combat and cannot be the target of Sustain Damage cancellation.

mech.name: Iron Sentinel
mech.ability: While this unit is on a planet, enemy bombardment against that planet produces 1 fewer hit (minimum 0). If this unit is destroyed, produce 2 hits against the attacking force before it is removed.

promissory.name: Galvanize Compact
promissory.text: When your system is attacked: Last Bastion may immediately move up to 3 ships to your system without activating it. Those ships fight as allies for this combat. Then, return this card to Last Bastion.

breakthrough.name: Bastion's Last Gift
breakthrough.text: Once per game, after you lose a combat, you may immediately replace all destroyed units with fresh units from your reinforcements, placed in your home system or any system you control.

agent.name: Warden Orak
agent.text: When a player would lose a space combat: You may exhaust this card. If you do, that player may retreat their surviving ships to any system they control instead of only adjacent systems.

commander.name: Marshal Voss
commander.text: Your ships have +1 movement when moving toward threatened systems (systems containing enemy ships or activated tokens). When you Galvanize, your ships move without spending command tokens.
commander.unlock: Successfully defend an ally's system 3 times using Galvanize.

hero.name: The Last Defender
hero.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 system. For the rest of the round, no player may activate that system. All ships that attempt to move to that system this round are destroyed.
hero.unlock: Defend your home system successfully 3 times.

name: The Ral Nel Consortium
lore: The Ral Nel are a mercantile civilization whose genius lies not in military innovation but in engineering miniaturization. Where other races build flagships the size of moons, the Ral Nel build destroyers with the firepower of dreadnoughts. Their Consortium is less a government and more a shareholders' agreement — every captain is an investor, every battle is a cost-benefit analysis, and every peace treaty is a market opportunity.

Their Miniaturization doctrine has produced remarkable results: Ral Nel destroyers carry Space Cannon batteries that larger ships would envy, and their fighters are equipped with electronics suites that make them effective scouts and interceptors far beyond their apparent class. The Consortium's motto is "Why build a hammer when a scalpel works better and costs less?"

The Ral Nel have no particular ideology regarding galactic governance. They want access to Mecatol Rex because whoever controls it controls the largest trading hub in the galaxy. The Galactic Council is, to the Consortium's board of directors, simply the most important regulatory body in known space — and they intend to own a majority stake in it.

abilities.name: Miniaturization
abilities.text: Your Destroyers have SPACE CANNON 7 (×2) and ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 8 (×3). They may fire Space Cannon from adjacent systems as if they were PDS units.

abilities.name: Shareholders
abilities.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each player who controls fewer planets than you. Other players may purchase "shares" (pay you 2 trade goods) to gain 1 command token during the production step.

abilities.name: Cost Efficiency
abilities.text: When you produce units, reduce the total production cost by 1 (minimum 1). When you produce 3 or more destroyers in a single production, produce 1 additional destroyer for free.

abilities.name: Market Access
abilities.text: You may use the secondary ability of the TRADE Strategy Card without spending a command token. When you do, gain 1 additional trade good.

factionTech.name: Miniaturized Space Cannon
factionTech.ability: Your Destroyers' Space Cannon range is increased to 2 systems. Your fighters gain ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 9 (×2).

factionTech.name: Consortium Logistics
factionTech.ability: Once per action phase, you may move up to 3 of your Destroyers to any system you control without activating it.

factionTech.name: Advanced Miniaturization
factionTech.ability: Your Destroyers now function as Dreadnoughts for all game purposes (same combat stats, Sustain Damage, Bombardment) while still counting as Destroyers for fleet pool and production purposes.

factionTech.name: Hostile Acquisition
factionTech.ability: After you win a combat, you may take 1 of the loser's action cards or command tokens instead of a trade good.

flagship.name: The Consortium Blade
flagship.ability: This ship counts as 3 Destroyers for all ability purposes. It has SPACE CANNON 6 (×4) and ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 7 (×4). When it is in a system, your other Destroyers in the same system gain +1 to all combat rolls.

mech.name: Miniaturized Titan
mech.ability: BOMBARDMENT 6 (×2). This unit counts as a Space Dock for production purposes: you may produce 1 unit per round at this unit's planet, paying normal costs.

promissory.name: Consortium Contract
promissory.text: When you produce units: You may produce up to 3 additional units without paying resources. After production, gain 1 trade good for each free unit produced. Then, return this card to the Ral Nel Consortium.

breakthrough.name: Market Cornering
breakthrough.text: Once per Status Phase, when another player replenishes commodities, you may take 1 of their commodities for free. Gain 1 command token for each player who does not replenish commodities this Status Phase.

agent.name: Trade Factor Neln
agent.text: When a player uses the TRADE Strategy Card: You may exhaust this card. If you do, gain 2 trade goods. That player also gains 1 trade good.

commander.name: CEO Ralneth
commander.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each player with fewer trade goods than you. You may spend 3 trade goods to gain 1 victory point.
commander.unlock: Accumulate 10 trade goods at one time.

hero.name: The Board
hero.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card. Offer each other player a deal: they may give you 2 trade goods to take control of 1 of your non-home planets. For each player who declines, move 1 of your ships to their home system.
hero.unlock: Complete 5 transactions (trade deals) with different players.

name: The Augurs of Ilyxum
lore: The Augurs of Ilyxum are an ancient order of seers whose civilization was built entirely around the practice of prophetic divination. For millennia, pilgrims traveled from across the galaxy to receive their visions, and the Augurs charged handsomely — not in currency, but in secrets. They know more about the inner workings of every major civilization than those civilizations know about themselves.

Their technology is a strange fusion of mysticism and hard science: crystalline arrays tuned to detect quantum probability fluctuations, interpreted by generations of trained seers into actionable intelligence. The Augurs do not see the future with perfect clarity, but they see it well enough to never be truly surprised by anything.

As they move toward Mecatol Rex, the Augurs carry with them the weight of a prophecy they have never shared publicly: one faction will achieve galactic dominance, and its name is written in their oldest texts. Whether that faction is themselves or another is the one question their seers refuse to answer.

abilities.name: Prophetic Vision
abilities.text: At the start of each Status Phase, look at the top 2 secret objectives in the deck. You may keep 1 and return the other to the top or bottom.

abilities.name: Foreknowledge
abilities.text: When another player reveals a secret objective, you may gain 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Ordained Path
abilities.text: Once per round, after another player scores an objective, you may move 1 command token from your strategy pool to your tactic pool.

abilities.name: The Reading
abilities.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Look at 1 other player's hand of action cards. You may swap 1 card from their hand with 1 card from yours.

factionTech.name: Probability Engine
factionTech.ability: At the start of each round, roll 1 die. On a 6+, gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Foresight Array
factionTech.ability: Before any player activates a system, you may spend 1 command token to look at their hand of action cards.

factionTech.name: Temporal Insight
factionTech.ability: Once per game, after any player scores an objective, you may immediately score 1 public objective you meet, without it counting against the normal scoring limit.

factionTech.name: Ilyxum Oracle
factionTech.ability: When you score a secret objective, gain 1 additional victory point. You may hold 4 secret objectives instead of 3.

flagship.name: Eye of Fate
flagship.ability: At the start of each round of space combat, you may look at your opponent's action cards. You may discard 1 of their action cards.

mech.name: Oracle Sentinel
mech.ability: Before ground combat begins, look at your opponent's action cards. You may discard 1.

promissory.name: Ilyxum Prophecy
promissory.text: When you score an objective: You may immediately draw 1 secret objective. Return this card to the Augurs player.

breakthrough.name: Fated Hour
breakthrough.text: Once per game, you may look at all public objectives not yet scored and choose 1. That objective is immediately available to score this round.

agent.name: High Augur Thessaly
agent.text: When a player draws secret objectives: You may exhaust this card. That player draws 1 additional secret objective, then passes 1 secret objective of their choice to you.

commander.name: Seer Orynth
commander.text: You may hold 1 additional action card. When you play an action card, gain 1 trade good.
commander.unlock: Score 3 secret objectives.

hero.name: The Grand Prophecy
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Name 1 public objective. If that objective is scored this round by any player, gain 2 victory points. If it is not scored, lose 1 victory point.

name: The Bentor Conglomerate
lore: The Bentor Conglomerate began as a consortium of artifact dealers who discovered that the relics scattered across the galaxy were not merely valuable curios — they were functional technology from a civilization that predated the current galactic order by hundreds of thousands of years. The Bentor have built their entire economy around acquiring, studying, and selectively weaponizing these ancient devices.

Their home system sits atop one of the galaxy's richest concentrations of pre-galactic ruins, and their explorers range far and wide in search of new dig sites. A Bentor expedition ship is one of the most dangerous things in the galaxy — not because of its weapons, but because of what it might find.

The Conglomerate is led not by a single executive but by a rotating council of the seven most successful artifact dealers of the current generation. Each brings their own obsession and specialty to the table, and the resulting governance is chaotic but effective. They want Mecatol Rex because the planet's depths are rumored to hold the grandest artifact of all — whatever the Lazax left behind when they died.

abilities.name: Awaken the Archive
abilities.text: When you explore a planet, you may gain 1 additional relic fragment of any type.

abilities.name: Artifact Mastery
abilities.text: When you purge relic fragments to gain a relic, you may purge 1 fewer fragment (minimum 1). You may hold up to 4 relic fragments of each type.

abilities.name: Deep Survey
abilities.text: Action: Exhaust 1 planet you control. Explore that planet as if it had not yet been explored. You may use this ability only once per planet.

abilities.name: Ancient Power
abilities.text: When you gain a relic, gain 1 trade good. When you use a relic's ability, you may gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Excavation Drones
factionTech.ability: When you explore a planet, produce 1 hit against any 1 enemy ground force on that planet.

factionTech.name: Relic Amplifier
factionTech.ability: When you use a relic's ability, you may apply its effect twice.

factionTech.name: Ancient Weapons Cache
factionTech.ability: Your ships gain +1 combat when attacking planets with attachment tokens.

factionTech.name: Progenitor Array
factionTech.ability: Once per round, you may purge 1 relic to gain 1 of its effects and 2 trade goods.

flagship.name: The Grand Archive
flagship.ability: When a planet in this system is explored, you may gain the relic fragment instead of the active player. Gain 1 trade good for each relic fragment gained this way.

mech.name: Excavator Mech
mech.ability: When this unit is on a planet, you may exhaust it to explore that planet (ignoring previous exploration). Gain 1 additional trade good from any exploration result.

promissory.name: Bentor Survey Rights
promissory.text: When you explore a planet: You may pass this card to the Bentor player. They gain 1 additional relic fragment. They give you 1 trade good. Return this card to the Bentor player.

breakthrough.name: Relic Surge
breakthrough.text: When you gain a relic this round, gain 1 command token. At the start of each Status Phase, if you control 3+ relics, gain 1 additional victory point (this bonus does not stack).

agent.name: Curator Fethyl
agent.text: When a player explores a planet: You may exhaust this card. That player may gain 1 additional relic fragment. They give you 1 trade good.

commander.name: Dealer Praxis
commander.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 relic fragment of your choice for each 3 planets you control.
commander.unlock: Control 3 relics simultaneously.

hero.name: The Grand Curator
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Gain 1 relic immediately (choose from top 3 in the relic deck). Then explore 1 planet you control for free.

name: The Celdauri Trade Confederation
lore: The Celdauri Trade Confederation is the galaxy's oldest surviving commercial institution — a network of merchant houses, shipping lanes, and financial instruments that predates most current civilizations by several centuries. They didn't build their power through military conquest; they built it through the patient accumulation of trade agreements, exclusive contracts, and carefully cultivated debts.

Every major civilization in the galaxy owes the Celdauri something. Whether it's a line of credit extended during a resource shortage, a shipment of critical materials delivered at cost during a war, or simply a favor given without expectation of immediate return — the Confederation keeps meticulous records. They are, in the words of one galactic historian, "the most dangerous civilization in the galaxy, because they never lose track of what you owe them."

Their move toward Mecatol Rex is less a military campaign than a collection effort. The Galactic Council has outstanding obligations to the Confederation that go back generations, and the Celdauri have decided that the time to collect has finally come.

abilities.name: Trade Network
abilities.text: When you use the secondary ability of the TRADE Strategy Card, gain 1 additional trade good. When another player uses the primary ability of the TRADE Strategy Card, you may also replenish your commodities.

abilities.name: Favorable Terms
abilities.text: When you make a transaction with another player, both players gain 1 additional trade good.

abilities.name: Debt Collection
abilities.text: At the start of each Status Phase, for each promissory note you hold from other players, gain 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Market Manipulation
abilities.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 player. They must immediately complete a transaction with you (trade goods for commodities) or lose 1 command token from their fleet pool.

factionTech.name: Celdauri Exchange
factionTech.ability: You may spend trade goods as if they were commodities. When you convert commodities to trade goods, gain 1 additional trade good.

factionTech.name: Cartel Logistics
factionTech.ability: Your Carriers have +1 capacity. When you move a Carrier through a system containing another player's ships, you may trade 1 commodity with that player without activating the system.

factionTech.name: Economic Leverage
factionTech.ability: When you spend trade goods to resolve an action card or tech, gain 1 additional commodity.

factionTech.name: Celdauri Monopoly
factionTech.ability: Once per game, you may declare a Monopoly on 1 resource type. Until your next turn, all players must pay you 1 trade good to produce units of that type.

flagship.name: The Golden Ledger
flagship.ability: When this ship is in a system with another player's ships, you may make a transaction with that player without spending a command token. Gain 1 trade good for each transaction made in this system.

mech.name: Enforcer Unit
mech.ability: When a player refuses to make a transaction with you, this unit may produce 2 automatic hits against that player's nearest ships.

promissory.name: Celdauri Credit Line
promissory.text: At any time: Gain 3 trade goods immediately. At the start of the next 3 Status Phases, lose 1 trade good. Return this card to the Celdauri player when the debt is paid.

breakthrough.name: Market Cornering
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when another player replenishes commodities, gain 1 trade good. If you have the most trade goods of any player, gain 1 command token at the start of each Status Phase.

agent.name: Factor Aelindra
agent.text: When a player uses the TRADE Strategy Card: You may exhaust this card. Gain 2 trade goods. That player also gains 1 trade good.

commander.name: Cartel Lord Thessan
commander.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each player who owes you a promissory note. Transactions you initiate always produce 1 additional trade good for you.
commander.unlock: Hold promissory notes from 3 different players simultaneously.

hero.name: The Grand Accord
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. All players must make a transaction with you this round or lose 1 VP. The value of each transaction is set by you (minimum 1 trade good).

name: The Cheiran Hordes
lore: The Cheiran are a species of massive, insectoid warriors who have spent their entire evolutionary history competing in conditions of extreme scarcity. Their homeworld's resources were so limited that warfare became a constant, and those who survived became something extraordinary — beings for whom combat is not just a strategy but a biological imperative.

The Cheiran Hordes do not have a government in the traditional sense. They have a Warlord — a champion chosen through ritual combat every generation — and beneath the Warlord, a hierarchy of warriors ranked by combat prowess. Diplomacy is viewed as a form of weakness, acceptable only as a temporary measure before the inevitable clash.

What makes the Hordes particularly terrifying is not just their individual ferocity but their tactical sophistication. Centuries of constant warfare have produced generals of extraordinary skill, and their unit doctrines are among the most refined in the galaxy. The Cheiran do not simply charge into battle; they calculate exactly how to destroy you before you realize the fight has started.

name: The Dih-Mohn Flotilla
lore: The Dih-Mohn are a species of fluid, bioluminescent organisms who evolved in the ocean depths of a world with virtually no land mass. When they first reached space, they didn't build habitats — they built ships so large that entire communities could live within them indefinitely. The concept of a permanent home planet is alien to the Dih-Mohn; their home is their fleet.

The Flotilla is not a nation but a migration — an ever-moving convoy of massive generation ships, warships, and support vessels that sweeps through systems like a traveling city. They consume what they need, trade what they don't, and move on. Attempts by other factions to pin them down militarily have consistently failed; by the time the enemy arrives, the Flotilla has moved.

What drives the Dih-Mohn toward Mecatol Rex is not conquest but curiosity. The Galactic Council represents the closest thing to a fixed community the Dih-Mohn have ever encountered, and they want to understand it. They also, if being honest, want to own it — a fixed point around which even their perpetual migration might one day orbit.

hero.name: The Living Tide

name: The Edyn Mandate
lore: The Edyn Mandate governs its territories through an elaborate system of bureaucratic law so comprehensive that it covers virtually every conceivable situation. Their legal code is the most extensive in the galaxy, and their administrative apparatus is so efficient that conquered territories often request to remain under Edyn governance even after Edyn military forces withdraw.

This is not, as outsiders often assume, evidence of benevolence. The Edyn legal system is designed to be impossible to fully comply with, ensuring that every citizen and vassal state is perpetually in some minor violation that can be selectively enforced when politically convenient. They rule through the constant threat of prosecution, not through direct force.

The Mandate's expansion toward Mecatol Rex is being pursued through legal channels — specifically, a 47,000-page legal brief arguing that the current Galactic Council is operating in violation of its own founding charter, and that the Edyn Mandate, as the aggrieved party, is entitled to administer a "caretaker government" until the matter is resolved. Several smaller factions have already accepted this argument.

hero.name: The Grand Mandate

name: The Florzen Profiteers
lore: The Florzen Profiteers are the galaxy's most cheerfully amoral opportunists. They have no particular ideology, no territorial ambitions beyond what's immediately profitable, and no enemies — only future business partners who don't know it yet. They thrive in the chaos of galactic warfare not by fighting but by selling to both sides.

Their business model is simple: follow the conflict, sell the supplies, scavenge the wrecks. A Florzen supply convoy arriving in a war zone is simultaneously welcomed and despised — welcomed because it brings critical materiel, despised because the Florzen will happily supply the enemy next week if the price is right. They have never been found to have any true loyalty beyond their profit margins.

The Florzen's move toward Mecatol Rex is driven by pure calculation: whoever controls the galactic capital will control the largest single market in existence, and the Florzen intend to be the preferred supplier to that market. Whether that means supporting its current rulers, helping them lose, or simply buying the whole thing outright is, as always, purely a matter of price.

name: The Free Systems Compact
lore: The Free Systems Compact is a voluntary federation of small systems that individually lack the power to survive in a galaxy of empires, but collectively represent a formidable political and economic force. They joined together not out of shared culture or history, but out of shared necessity — and discovered, to their surprise, that they actually liked each other.

The Compact's political model is based on radical consensus-building: every member system has an equal vote on Compact decisions, from grand strategy to trade policy to which flavor of rations to stockpile. This sounds inefficient, and it often is, but it also means that every Compact decision has the genuine support of every member, which produces a remarkable degree of coordinated action when the decision is finally made.

Their expansion toward Mecatol Rex is driven by the simple democratic argument that a galactic government should represent all systems, not just the powerful ones. The Compact has been presenting this argument in the Galactic Council for decades. Now they've decided to back the argument with warships.

hero.name: The Grand Referendum

name: The Ghemina Raiders
lore: The Ghemina Raiders are the galaxy's most feared pirates — not because they are individually the strongest, but because they are the most disciplined and most ruthless. Unlike the romantic notion of space piracy, the Ghemina operate with military precision: intelligence networks that map every trade route, supply chain analysis to identify the highest-value targets, and strike forces optimized for maximum damage and immediate withdrawal.

Their home system is a hidden fortress-base that even their trading partners don't know the exact location of. The Ghemina have maintained this secrecy for generations by the simple expedient of killing anyone who threatens to reveal it. They are not cruel for cruelty's sake — they are simply committed to operational security at a level that most civilizations find alarming.

The Raiders' interest in Mecatol Rex is tactical: the planet sits at the center of the galaxy's most important trade routes. Controlling it would give them intelligence on every significant shipment in known space. They don't particularly want to rule the galaxy; they want to rob it more efficiently.

name: The Ghoti Wayfarers
lore: The Ghoti Wayfarers have no home planets. This is not a tragedy or a limitation — it is their entire identity. An aquatic species that evolved in the interstellar medium itself (or so their mythology claims), the Ghoti have never understood the concept of owning a piece of rock. They own their ships, their routes, and the space between the stars.

Their fleet is their civilization. Every Ghoti is born on a ship, raised on a ship, and will die on a ship. Their social structure is organized around fleets rather than planets, and their "cities" are massive convoy formations that travel the galaxy in patterns that have been followed for thousands of years. The routes themselves are sacred.

The Ghoti's presence in the galactic conflict is motivated by self-preservation: the constant territorial warfare threatens the routes they have traveled for millennia. They don't want to rule the galaxy, but they do want the right to sail wherever they choose, and Mecatol Rex represents the most direct path to securing that right permanently.

name: The Gledge Union
lore: The Gledge Union is the galaxy's most powerful organized labor movement, elevated to the status of an interstellar civilization. What began as a series of strikes in the industrial heartland of the Gledge system three centuries ago has grown into a governing coalition that controls an entire sector through the simple principle that nothing gets built without the Union's say-so.

The Union's power comes not from military force but from its absolute control over the means of production. Every factory, every shipyard, every mine in Union territory operates under a Gledge labor contract, and the Union has never hesitated to call a general strike when its interests are threatened. The record speaks for itself: seven attempts to militarily subjugate the Union have ended in the same way, with attacking forces unable to get spare parts for their ships.

They seek Mecatol Rex because the Galactic Council's labor regulations have been, in the Union's view, systematically unfair for centuries. They want a seat at the table. And if they can't get a seat, they'll build their own table — and make sure everyone else has to buy theirs from them.

hero.name: The Grand Strike

name: The Kollecc Society
lore: The Kollecc Society is a civilization organized around the singular obsession of collection. Over millennia, what began as a cultural predisposition toward accumulation has been refined into a philosophy, a governance model, and ultimately a military doctrine. The Kollecc collect everything: art, technology, people, planets, and power.

Their home system is a museum of galactic history so comprehensive that most other civilizations send diplomatic missions just to study it. The Kollecc charge admission. They also charge for access to their archives, their technological specimens, and their unprecedented catalog of functional alien artifacts. Their economy runs on the simple principle that knowledge and possession of things is worth more than the things themselves.
The Kollecc want Mecatol Rex for the same reason they want everything: it is the most historically significant object in the galaxy, and they don't have it yet.
