abilities.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 player. You may trade any number of relics or technologies with that player. You gain 1 trade good for each item traded.

abilities.name: Specimen Study
abilities.text: When you destroy an enemy flagship or mech, gain 1 of that faction's faction technologies temporarily (for the rest of the game, as a copy).

factionTech.name: Acquisition Engine
factionTech.ability: When you explore a planet, you may gain the relic fragment AND the normal exploration result.

factionTech.name: Collection Protocol
factionTech.ability: You may hold 6 relic fragments of each type. When you purge fragments to gain a relic, you may choose from the top 3 relics in the deck.

factionTech.name: Specimen Vault
factionTech.ability: You may hold copies of up to 2 other factions' faction technologies (gained through Specimen Study). These function as your own technologies.

factionTech.name: Grand Museum
factionTech.ability: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 VP for each 5 unique items you have collected (relics, faction technologies, promissory notes from different factions).

flagship.name: The Collection Ship
flagship.ability: When a unit is destroyed in this system, you may claim it as a "specimen." Gain 1 trade good per specimen. When you have 10+ specimens, gain 1 VP.

mech.name: Acquisition Mech
mech.ability: When this unit wins ground combat, take 1 technology or relic fragment from the losing player's planet (if any are present).

promissory.name: Kollecc Exhibition Rights
promissory.text: When you score an objective: The Kollecc player gains 1 trade good. You may draw 1 action card. Return this card to the Kollecc player.

breakthrough.name: Grand Collection
breakthrough.text: Once per game, gain 1 copy of any technology you do not own (ignoring prerequisites and faction restrictions). Gain 1 trade good for each unique technology you own beyond 8.

agent.name: Curator Selyx
agent.text: When a player gains a relic: You may exhaust this card. You gain 1 relic fragment of any type.

commander.name: Collector Prime
commander.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each unique faction represented by items you have collected.
commander.unlock: Collect items from 4 different factions.

hero.name: The Grand Acquisition
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Take 1 technology from any player and add it to your collection permanently. Pay that player 2 trade goods.

name: The Kortali Tribunal
lore: The Kortali Tribunal is the galaxy's self-appointed enforcement arm, a civilization that has built its identity around the concept of cosmic justice. They are not, strictly speaking, benevolent — the Tribunal's justice tends to favor those who can afford the finest advocates and who have made appropriate contributions to Tribunal scholarship funds. But they are consistent, and consistency counts for something in a galaxy this chaotic.

The Tribunal emerged from a period of particularly brutal interstellar lawlessness when the previous galactic government collapsed. The Kortali stepped into the vacuum, not out of altruism, but because the chaos was bad for business (they were merchants at the time). Three centuries later, they are no longer merchants — they are judges, and the distinction matters enormously to them.

Their campaign toward Mecatol Rex is, technically speaking, a legal enforcement action. The Tribunal has ruled that the current galactic order is in violation of seventeen separate legal frameworks, and they are advancing to serve the appropriate paperwork. The fleet of warships accompanying the process servers is, they insist, merely standard procedure.

abilities.name: Judgment
abilities.text: When another player breaks a deal or agreement, you may immediately exhaust 1 of their planets (chosen by you) as a "penalty." They gain 1 trade good as restitution.

abilities.name: Due Process
abilities.text: Before each Agenda Phase, you may name 1 player as being "under review." That player cannot vote on the first agenda of the Agenda Phase.

abilities.name: Legal Precedent
abilities.text: When you vote on an agenda, if the same outcome you chose won the previous agenda, you gain 2 additional votes.

abilities.name: Tribunal Enforcement
abilities.text: When you win a combat in another player's home system, gain 2 victory points. These points cannot be lost.

factionTech.name: Enforcement Fleet
factionTech.ability: Your ships have +1 combat when attacking players who have broken deals this game. Track broken deals with tokens.

factionTech.name: Writ of Execution
factionTech.ability: Action: Exhaust this card. Name 1 player and 1 system. That player cannot activate that system this round.

factionTech.name: Tribunal Mandate
factionTech.ability: When you win an agenda vote, choose 1 player. They must exhaust 1 planet or lose 1 trade good.

factionTech.name: Supreme Judgment
factionTech.ability: Once per game, after winning a combat, you may immediately score 1 secret objective from your hand without meeting its requirements.

flagship.name: The Final Verdict
flagship.ability: While this ship is in a system, no player may make transactions or deals. Gain 1 trade good at the start of each combat round this ship participates in.

mech.name: Arbiter Mech
mech.ability: While this unit is on a planet, the planet cannot be exhausted by enemy abilities. PLANETARY SHIELD.

promissory.name: Tribunal Immunity
promissory.text: While you hold this card, you are immune to Judgment penalties from the Kortali player. In exchange, the Kortali player may vote alongside you on 1 agenda. Return this card to the Kortali player.

breakthrough.name: Justice Served
breakthrough.text: When you defeat a player in combat for the first time this game, gain 1 VP and 2 trade goods. This can only trigger once per player per game.

agent.name: Judge Advocate Prynn
agent.text: When a player votes on an agenda: You may exhaust this card. That player must vote for the same outcome as their last vote, or lose 1 command token.

commander.name: Chief Justice Varan
commander.text: Your votes during agendas cannot be cancelled or redirected. When you win an agenda, gain 1 trade good for each player who voted against you.
commander.unlock: Win 4 agenda votes.

hero.name: The Grand Tribunal
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 player. They must immediately comply with 1 demand you make (gain/lose 1 trade good, exhaust 1 planet, or move 1 ship out of a system). They cannot refuse.

name: The Kyro Sodality
lore: The Kyro Sodality is a religious order that spans dozens of systems and claims millions of adherents across hundreds of species. Their faith centers on the concept of the Galactic Pilgrimage — a journey across all the sacred sites of the galaxy that culminates at Mecatol Rex, which the Sodality believes is the location where the divine pattern underlying all of existence is most clearly visible.

For most of their history, the Sodality sought to make this pilgrimage peacefully. But the galaxy is a dangerous place, and sacred sites have a tendency to get militarized. The Kyro have therefore spent the last century building a military that can protect pilgrims — and, when necessary, liberate sacred sites from those who desecrate them.

Their war theology is elaborate: violence is permitted when in service of the sacred, forbidden when motivated by greed or power-seeking. Conveniently, the Sodality's leadership has determined that nearly every military action they need to take to reach Mecatol Rex qualifies as sacred. This determination has been received with varying degrees of credulity by the factions they've conquered.

abilities.name: Sacred Pilgrimage
abilities.text: When you move ships toward Mecatol Rex (closer by system count), those ships have +1 movement. When you control Mecatol Rex, gain 1 additional VP.

abilities.name: Holy War
abilities.text: When you attack a player who controls a Cultural planet you previously controlled, your units gain +1 combat in that system.

abilities.name: Converts
abilities.text: When you win a ground combat on a Cultural planet, place 1 Convert token on it. Planets with Convert tokens produce 1 additional influence.

abilities.name: Blessed Armament
abilities.text: Your mechs have Sustain Damage. When a mech uses Sustain Damage in defense of a Cultural planet, restore it at no cost.

factionTech.name: Sacred Rites
factionTech.ability: Once per round, when you control a Cultural planet, you may exhaust it to place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on an adjacent planet you control.

factionTech.name: Crusade Doctrine
factionTech.ability: Your ships attacking systems with Cultural planets gain +1 combat. When you capture a Cultural planet, gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Temple Guard
factionTech.ability: Your PDS units have SPACE CANNON 4 (×3). PDS units defending Cultural planets gain +2 to their Space Cannon rolls.

factionTech.name: Pilgrimage Fleet
factionTech.ability: Your ships have +1 movement when moving toward Mecatol Rex or Cultural planets. When you take Mecatol Rex, gain 1 additional relic.

flagship.name: The Sacred Crusade
flagship.ability: When this ship is in a system with a Cultural planet, all your units in that system gain +1 combat. Gain 1 influence when you capture a Cultural planet this ship helped attack.

mech.name: Temple Warden
mech.ability: While on a Cultural planet, this unit grants PLANETARY SHIELD and prevents BOMBARDMENT. Enemies must destroy this unit before they can invade the planet.

promissory.name: Blessing of the Sodality
promissory.text: When you win a combat: Your ground forces may immediately move to 1 adjacent planet and invade it without spending a command token. Return this card to the Kyro player.

breakthrough.name: Sacred Momentum
breakthrough.text: When you capture a Cultural planet, gain 1 command token. Once per game, when you capture Mecatol Rex, immediately score 1 secret objective from your hand.

agent.name: High Priest Navar
agent.text: When a player controls a Cultural planet: You may exhaust this card. Place 1 Convert token on that planet. The controlling player gains 1 trade good.

commander.name: Grand Crusader
commander.text: Your ships have +1 combat when in systems with Cultural planets. You gain 1 trade good for each Cultural planet you capture.
commander.unlock: Control 5 Cultural planets simultaneously.

hero.name: The Sacred Vision
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Move all your ships from any systems toward Mecatol Rex (1 system closer each). This movement does not spend command tokens.

name: The L'tokk Khrask
lore: The L'tokk Khrask are a species of massive reptilian warriors who have organized their society around the concept of the Worthy Hunt — a cultural framework that elevates the strongest predator to leadership and demands that any significant decision be settled through direct personal combat. Their Warlord is always the deadliest being in the civilization, proven through challenge.

Despite appearances, the L'tokk are not mindless brutes. Their tactical intelligence is exceptional, their engineering capabilities considerable, and their diplomatic tradition — the Worthy Parley — involves a nuanced ritual of offer, counter-offer, and binding oath that most other civilizations find surprisingly sophisticated. They simply resolve their final disagreements with combat rather than lawyers.

The L'tokk move toward Mecatol Rex because the Galactic Council represents the largest unchallenged prey in the galaxy. Their current Warlord, Khrask the Undefeated, has issued a formal Worthy Challenge to every faction currently competing for galactic dominance. By their cultural law, failure to respond is a forfeit. Most factions have yet to respond.

abilities.name: Worthy Challenge
abilities.text: Once per round, you may declare a Worthy Challenge against 1 enemy unit. Both units fight 1-on-1 (each rolls 1 die, adding their combat value). The loser is destroyed; the winner gains 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Apex Predator
abilities.text: Your mechs and infantry have +1 combat in ground combat. When your ground forces win combat without losing any units, gain 1 command token.

abilities.name: Honor Bound
abilities.text: You cannot be forced to retreat from combat. Your units fight to the last and gain +1 combat in the final round of any combat.

abilities.name: Pack Tactics
abilities.text: When 3 or more of your mechs are in the same system, they each gain +1 combat.

factionTech.name: Apex Armor
factionTech.ability: Your mechs have combat 5 (×2) and Sustain Damage. When a mech destroys a unit, it produces 1 additional hit against the next enemy unit.

factionTech.name: Khrask Berserker
factionTech.ability: MECH UPGRADE — Your mechs gain BOMBARDMENT 7 (×3) and ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 8 (×2). In the first round of any combat, your mechs roll 1 additional die.

factionTech.name: Predator Sense
factionTech.ability: Before any combat, you may look at your opponent's combat units' stats. Your units gain +1 combat against any unit type you have studied this game.

factionTech.name: Worthy Arsenal
factionTech.ability: Your mechs fight in space combat as well as ground combat. In space, treat mechs as Fighters with combat 6 (×2).

flagship.name: The Khrask Titan
flagship.ability: This ship may carry 8 mechs/infantry. When your ground forces invade from this ship, they gain +2 combat in the first round.

mech.name: L'tokk Warrior
mech.ability: This unit may issue Worthy Challenges. When this unit wins a Worthy Challenge, it gains +1 combat permanently (maximum +3 total across the game per unit).

promissory.name: Khrask's Blessing
promissory.text: Before a ground combat: Your mechs in this combat gain +2 combat this round. Return this card to the L'tokk player.

breakthrough.name: Predator's Momentum
breakthrough.text: When your mechs destroy 3+ enemy units in a single combat round, gain 1 command token and immediately move your surviving mechs to an adjacent planet.

agent.name: Hunt-Master Vreth
agent.text: When a player initiates ground combat: You may exhaust this card. Add 2 L'tokk mechs to that combat as mercenaries for the attacking player.

commander.name: Warlord Khrask
commander.text: Your mechs have combat 4 (×3) at all times. When you win a ground combat using only mechs, immediately place 1 additional mech on that planet.
commander.unlock: Win 5 ground combats using only mechs.

hero.name: The Undefeated
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Issue Worthy Challenges to all enemy mechs and infantry on 1 planet. Your champion mech fights each in sequence (restore health between fights). Win all combats to claim the planet instantly.

name: The Lanefir Remnants
lore: The Lanefir Remnants are what's left of a civilization that was nearly destroyed two centuries ago by a catastrophe whose nature they refuse to discuss publicly. What is known: the Lanefir once controlled twelve systems and a population of billions. They now control three systems and a population measured in millions. What survived was the most adaptable, the most determined, and — some would say — the most dangerous subset of their original civilization.

Survival at all costs has become the Lanefir's core value, expressed through military adaptability that other factions find unsettling. The Remnants field whatever weapons are available, repair ships with whatever materials are on hand, and deploy tactics that would be considered unconventional by most military traditions. They don't have a preferred way to fight; they have every way to fight.

The Lanefir seek Mecatol Rex because it represents the one thing their catastrophe taught them they needed most: security. A civilization cannot be destroyed if it controls the center of the galaxy. They intend to never be vulnerable again.

abilities.name: Adaptive Survival
abilities.text: Once per round, after you lose a combat, you may immediately repair all your surviving ships (remove all damage tokens) and move them 1 system for free.

abilities.name: Scrap Engineering
abilities.text: When an enemy ship is destroyed in a system you control, you may produce 1 fighter from your reinforcements in that system for free.

abilities.name: Remnant Determination
abilities.text: Your units in your home system cannot be destroyed by BOMBARDMENT or SPACE CANNON. They gain +1 combat in your home system.

abilities.name: Field Repair
abilities.text: At the start of each of your turns, you may repair 1 ship that used Sustain Damage last round.

factionTech.name: Salvage Protocol
factionTech.ability: When any ship is destroyed in a system you occupy, gain 1 trade good. When you gain 3 trade goods this way in one round, gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Remnant Armor
factionTech.ability: All your ships gain Sustain Damage. Your ships with Sustain Damage get +1 combat while damaged.

factionTech.name: Adaptive Tactics
factionTech.ability: Before each combat, choose 1: your units gain +1 combat, +1 movement this round, or produce 1 free unit after combat.

factionTech.name: Phoenix Protocol
factionTech.ability: Once per game, when your home system would fall, cancel that capture. Rebuild 3 ships in your home system from your reinforcements for free.

flagship.name: The Last Hope
flagship.ability: When this ship uses Sustain Damage, it gains +2 combat until repaired. When this ship would be destroyed, it instead survives with 1 hit point (once per combat).

mech.name: Salvage Walker
mech.ability: When this unit is destroyed, produce 2 infantry from your reinforcements on the same planet. BOMBARDMENT 8.

promissory.name: Remnant Alliance
promissory.text: When you are attacked: You may exhaust this card. The Lanefir player may move 2 ships to your system as allies. After the combat, they gain 1 trade good per enemy ship destroyed. Return this card.

breakthrough.name: Survivor's Resolve
breakthrough.text: When you lose a combat, gain 1 command token and 1 trade good. When you win a combat after losing the previous combat in the same system, gain 1 VP.

agent.name: Rebuilder Thar
agent.text: When a player loses ships in combat: You may exhaust this card. That player may produce 2 ships from their reinforcements in the combat system for half cost.

commander.name: Survivor General
commander.text: Your ships have Sustain Damage regardless of type. When any of your ships use Sustain Damage, gain 1 trade good.
commander.unlock: Recover from 5 combats using Sustain Damage.

hero.name: The Last Stand
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Your home system becomes immune to all attacks, BOMBARDMENT, and invasion for 2 rounds. Place 5 units of any type from your reinforcements in your home system for free.
hero.unlock: Successfully defend your home system 3 times.

name: The Li-Zho Dynasty
lore: The Li-Zho Dynasty is an ancient civilization of contemplative warriors whose culture is built on the principle of wu wei — action through non-action, victory through perfect alignment with the natural flow of events. Their military tradition is not about overwhelming force but about positioning: being exactly where they need to be, exactly when they need to be there, and allowing events to unfold in their favor.

Their philosophy produces an approach to galactic politics that other factions find simultaneously admirable and infuriating. The Li-Zho never seem to be in a hurry. They never seem to be threatened. They make patient, long-term investments in systems and relationships that other civilizations dismiss as irrelevant, and then those investments turn out to matter enormously at precisely the wrong moment for their opponents.

The Dynasty's move toward Mecatol Rex is, as always, patient. They have been positioning for this campaign for decades, and they are confident that when the time comes, everything will be in place. Whether that time is this century or next is, from their perspective, merely a detail.

abilities.name: Flowing Strike
abilities.text: Once per round, when you activate a system, you may move 1 additional ship into that system from any adjacent system at no cost.

abilities.name: Patient Positioning
abilities.text: At the end of each round, if you did not initiate any combat this round, gain 1 command token and 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Perfect Balance
abilities.text: When your resource total and influence total within a single system are equal, planets in that system produce 1 additional of each.

abilities.name: Harmonious Force
abilities.text: Your units in systems you have controlled for 2+ rounds gain +1 combat.

factionTech.name: Flow State
factionTech.ability: When your ships move, they may move through 1 additional system. Ships moved through systems leave no command tokens in those transit systems.

factionTech.name: Dynasty Doctrine
factionTech.ability: At the start of each round, look at the top strategy card. You may place it on the bottom of the deck.

factionTech.name: Harmonious Assembly
factionTech.ability: When you produce units, reduce the cost by 1 for each resource-influence balanced system you control.

factionTech.name: Wu Wei Mastery
factionTech.ability: Once per game, you may immediately score 1 public or secret objective without meeting its requirements, citing "perfect alignment."

flagship.name: Dynasty Star
flagship.ability: This ship may move through systems without activating them. When it arrives at its destination, it enters combat with +2 combat for the first round (the "surprise" bonus).

mech.name: Flowing Warrior
mech.ability: This unit may move between planets in the same system once per round. Each round it fights in a different planet, gain 1 trade good.

promissory.name: Dynasty Wisdom
promissory.text: When you are about to score an objective: You may score 1 additional objective this Status Phase. Return this card to the Li-Zho player.

breakthrough.name: Perfect Timing
breakthrough.text: Once per game, at the start of any player's turn, you may immediately take your turn instead (interrupting the normal order). After your turn, play resumes normally.

agent.name: Master Shen
agent.text: When a player passes their turn: You may exhaust this card. Gain 2 trade goods. The passing player gains 1 command token.

commander.name: Grand Marshal Xao
commander.text: When you move ships, you may move them through 2 additional systems. Your fleet never leaves command tokens in systems it passes through.
commander.unlock: Control planets in 6 different systems.

hero.name: The Perfect Flow
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. For this round, your ships and units may move freely to any system within 3 movement (ignoring normal movement rules). This costs no command tokens.
hero.unlock: Not initiate combat for 3 consecutive rounds.

name: The Mirveda Protectorate
lore: The Mirveda Protectorate was established with a simple mandate: protect the systems under its care from external threats. Three centuries later, that mandate has expanded considerably. The Protectorate now defines "external threats" quite broadly, and "systems under its care" even more broadly still. They are, in the most charitable reading, the galaxy's most well-armed peacekeepers.

The Protectorate's military doctrine is based on layered defense: every system they control is a fortress, every planet a strongpoint, every ship a mobile bunker. They believe that the best offense is a defense so impenetrable that no enemy will attempt to breach it, and their track record suggests this theory has merit. The last serious attempt to invade Mirveda territory ended with the attackers retreating after three weeks of fighting for the outermost system.

Their campaign toward Mecatol Rex is framed as a "protective intervention" — the Galactic Council, in the Protectorate's view, cannot adequately protect itself, and has therefore triggered the Protectorate's standing mandate to step in. The fact that this puts them in control of the most valuable real estate in the galaxy is, they insist, purely incidental.

abilities.name: Layered Defense
abilities.text: Your PDS units may fire at enemy ships 2 systems away (not just adjacent). When your PDS fires, it rolls 1 additional die.

abilities.name: Fortified Position
abilities.text: Your ships defending a system you control gain +1 combat. When defending your home system, all units gain +2 combat.

abilities.name: Shield Protocol
abilities.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 system you control. That system cannot be activated by any other player this round.

abilities.name: Protectorate Mandate
abilities.text: When you build a Space Dock, also place 1 PDS for free. Your Space Docks have PLANETARY SHIELD.

factionTech.name: Mirveda Barrier
factionTech.ability: Your PDS units have SPACE CANNON 4 (×4). When multiple PDS units fire at the same target, combine their rolls into a single pool.

factionTech.name: Shield Dome
factionTech.ability: Once per round, when an enemy activates a system you control, you may cancel 1 of their ships' hits against your PDS or Space Dock.

factionTech.name: Protector Network
factionTech.ability: Your PDS units in adjacent systems may combine their fire as if they were in the same system. Range extends to 2 systems.

factionTech.name: Inviolate Fortress
factionTech.ability: Your home system is immune to BOMBARDMENT and INVASION while you control Mecatol Rex or any Legendary planet.

flagship.name: The Iron Curtain
flagship.ability: This ship has SPACE CANNON 4 (×4). While in a system, treat all your PDS as if they were in this ship's system as well. Enemy ships in adjacent systems cannot use BOMBARDMENT.

mech.name: Fortress Mech
mech.ability: While on a planet, this unit has PLANETARY SHIELD and SPACE CANNON 6 (×2). It fires before ground combat begins.

promissory.name: Protectorate Shield
promissory.text: When you are invaded: Cancel 2 hits from the first round of ground combat. The Mirveda player gains 1 trade good. Return this card to the Mirveda player.

breakthrough.name: Iron Defense
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when an enemy activates a system you control, immediately fire all your PDS units in that and adjacent systems before combat begins. These are bonus shots and do not count against normal PDS usage.

agent.name: Warden-Prime Syla
agent.text: When a player builds a PDS: You may exhaust this card. That player may build 1 additional PDS for free. You gain 1 trade good.

commander.name: Shield-Marshal Orin
commander.text: Your PDS units have SPACE CANNON 3 (×5) and may fire at ships 3 systems away.
commander.unlock: Control 6 PDS units simultaneously.

hero.name: The Unbreakable Line
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. For this round, your systems cannot be activated by any player. All enemy ships that attempt to enter your systems are automatically destroyed.
hero.unlock: Successfully defend all your systems for 3 consecutive rounds.

name: The Monks of Kolume
lore: The Monks of Kolume are a paradox: an order of pacifist philosophers who command one of the most effective military forces in the galaxy. They resolved this paradox centuries ago through careful theological distinction: violence is not the problem, they concluded; attachment to violence is the problem. The Monks fight without malice, conquer without hatred, and destroy without cruelty — which, somehow, makes them more dangerous, not less.

Their home on the mountain-world of Kolume is both a monastery and a fortress, indistinguishable from the outside. Thousands of students travel there every generation to study the Kolume Way, and most of them return home as better people. A small percentage stay to become Monks, which means a small percentage return as something else entirely.

The Monks seek Mecatol Rex because their philosophical tradition holds that the center of the galaxy is where the universe is most balanced, and balance, they believe, is what the galaxy currently lacks. They intend to provide it. They have not specified what form this provision will take, which other factions find unnerving.

abilities.name: Detachment
abilities.text: Your units never receive negative modifiers from anomalies, hazards, or special terrain effects. Nebulae, gravity rifts, and supernovas affect your ships as open space.

abilities.name: Peaceful Purpose
abilities.text: If you have not initiated any combat this round, gain 1 trade good at the end of the round and draw 1 action card.

abilities.name: Mindful Assault
abilities.text: When you initiate a combat (not when defending), choose 1: your units gain +2 combat this round, or immediately end the combat in a draw with no casualties on either side.

abilities.name: Kolume Discipline
abilities.text: Your ground forces gain +1 combat for each consecutive round of ground combat (cumulative, reset each combat).

factionTech.name: Monastic Focus
factionTech.ability: Your units gain +1 combat when fighting in systems you have controlled for 2+ rounds.

factionTech.name: Way of Silence
factionTech.ability: When you do not play an action card during a round, gain 1 command token and 1 trade good.

factionTech.name: Kolume Strike
factionTech.ability: Once per combat, you may meditate (skip your unit rolls this round) to deal 3 automatic hits to the enemy next round.

factionTech.name: Transcendent Form
factionTech.ability: Your mechs have combat 4 (×3) and cannot be cancelled by enemy abilities. They ignore Sustain Damage (simply cannot be destroyed by Sustain).

flagship.name: Temple of War
flagship.ability: At the start of each space combat round, you may choose to meditate: deal 2 automatic hits to the enemy without rolling dice.

mech.name: Monk Warrior
mech.ability: In the first round of ground combat, this unit produces 2 automatic hits before dice are rolled. In subsequent rounds, it has combat 4 (×3).

promissory.name: Kolume Meditation
promissory.text: Before a combat: All combatants gain +1 combat this combat. Neither side may retreat. The Monks player gains 1 trade good per round of combat. Return this card.

breakthrough.name: Path of Mastery
breakthrough.text: When you win a combat without losing any units, gain 1 VP token (these are special and do not count against normal scoring, maximum 1 per game). Gain 1 command token.

agent.name: Elder Sova
agent.text: When a player initiates a combat: You may exhaust this card. Both players may choose to end the combat as a draw with no casualties. Both gain 1 trade good.

commander.name: Grand Master
commander.text: Your units produce 1 automatic hit in the first round of every combat (space and ground). This does not replace their normal rolls.
commander.unlock: Win 5 combats without losing any units.

hero.name: The Still Mind
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Immediately end all ongoing combats across the entire game board as draws. No units are destroyed. All fleets return to where they came from.

name: The Myko-Mentori
lore: The Myko-Mentori are a fungal collective consciousness that has been quietly spreading across the galaxy for millennia. Unlike other hive minds, the Myko-Mentori do not absorb other species — they infect them, introducing fungal spores that create a gentle neural link without destroying the host's consciousness. The result is a civilization of billions of nominally independent individuals who share a subtle but persistent connection to the Collective.

Most infected individuals don't know they're infected. The spores don't control; they merely suggest, nudge, and occasionally provide useful information at convenient moments. The Myko-Mentori have found that willing cooperation, even when it's subtly coerced, produces better results than direct domination.

The Collective's interest in Mecatol Rex is both practical and philosophical. Practically, controlling the galactic center would allow their spore distribution networks to reach every inhabited system within decades. Philosophically, they believe that true galactic peace requires connection — the kind of connection that only the Myko-Mentori's gentle neural interface can provide. The fact that this would make them the dominant consciousness in the galaxy is, they insist, merely a side effect.

abilities.name: Spore Spread
abilities.text: When you control a planet, place 1 Spore token on an adjacent uncontrolled planet. While a planet has a Spore token, you may produce infantry on it even without controlling it.

abilities.name: Neural Link
abilities.text: Once per round, you may look at 1 other player's action card hand. You may discard 1 card from their hand and they gain 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Fungal Network
abilities.text: Your planets connected by Spore tokens count as adjacent for movement purposes. You may move troops between connected planets for free.

abilities.name: Collective Mind
abilities.text: When you score a secret objective, draw 1 action card. When you have 3+ Spore tokens on the board, gain 1 additional action card per Status Phase.

factionTech.name: Rapid Sporulation
factionTech.ability: At the start of each round, place 1 Spore token on any planet within 2 systems of a planet you control. You may have up to 10 Spore tokens on the board.

factionTech.name: Mycelial Web
factionTech.ability: Planets with Spore tokens produce 1 additional resource when you exhaust them. When you gain control of a planet with a Spore token, gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Neural Domination
factionTech.ability: When a player uses an action card, you may pay 1 trade good to copy its effect for yourself.

factionTech.name: Hive Surge
factionTech.ability: Once per round, you may produce 3 infantry at any planet that has a Spore token, even if you don't control it. These infantry fight for you.

flagship.name: The Mother Spore
flagship.ability: At the end of each round, place 1 Spore token in every system this ship occupies or passes through. Spore tokens placed by this ship cannot be removed.

mech.name: Fungal Colossus
mech.ability: When this unit wins ground combat, place 1 Spore token on every adjacent planet. BOMBARDMENT 8.

promissory.name: Spore Blessing
promissory.text: When you score a public objective: Place 2 Spore tokens on planets you control. Gain 1 command token. Return this card to the Myko-Mentori player.

breakthrough.name: Hive Awakening
breakthrough.text: Once per game, when you have 8+ Spore tokens on the board, you may convert all Spore tokens into infantry on those planets (even those you don't control). These infantry fight for you.

agent.name: Spore-Master Vel
agent.text: When a player explores a planet: You may exhaust this card. Place 1 Spore token on that planet. You may produce 1 infantry there for free.

commander.name: Neural Hub
commander.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each planet with a Spore token. You may convert Spore tokens into infantry at will (1 token = 2 infantry).
commander.unlock: Have 6 Spore tokens on the board simultaneously.

hero.name: The Grand Network
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Convert all Spore tokens on the board into infantry fighting for you. For this round, treat all planets with Spore tokens as planets you control for scoring purposes.
hero.unlock: Have 10 Spore tokens on the board.

name: The Nivyn Star Kings
lore: The Nivyn Star Kings are an ancient aristocracy that traces its lineage directly to the first civilizations to achieve spaceflight in this arm of the galaxy. They are not, technically, immortal — but their life-extension technologies are sufficiently advanced that the ruling dynasty has been in continuous operation for over twelve hundred years, with some current council members who remember the original Galactic Council.

Their culture is obsessed with stellar phenomena: nebulae, supernovas, gravity wells, and the slow dance of binary star systems. The Star Kings believe that the structures of the cosmos are legible to those patient enough to read them, and that understanding stellar phenomena grants strategic insight that more hurried civilizations simply lack.

Their military is built around the exploitation of stellar anomalies. Where other factions see obstacles, the Nivyn see advantages. Their fleets thrive in conditions that would shred ordinary vessels, and their commanders are trained to use the battlefield's natural features as weapons. The Star Kings don't conquer despite the dangers of space; they conquer because of them.

abilities.name: Stellar Mastery
abilities.text: Your ships are not affected by any anomaly (nebula, gravity rift, supernova, asteroid field). Instead, your ships gain +1 combat in each anomaly type they are in.

abilities.name: Royal Decree
abilities.text: At the start of each Agenda Phase, choose 1 other player. That player must give you 1 trade good before casting votes. You gain votes equal to trade goods received.

abilities.name: Star Sight
abilities.text: At the start of each round, you may look at the top action card in the deck. You may place it on the bottom.

abilities.name: Nova Lance
abilities.text: Once per round, after entering a system containing a star anomaly, your flagship may fire SPACE CANNON 4 (×3) using the star's energy.

factionTech.name: Stellar Navigation
factionTech.ability: Your ships' movement is not reduced by anomalies. Ships moving through anomalies gain +1 additional movement.

factionTech.name: Royal Armament
factionTech.ability: Your Cruisers and Dreadnoughts have +1 combat. In nebulae, they gain an additional +1 combat (total +2).

factionTech.name: Star Forge
factionTech.ability: When you produce units in a system with an anomaly, produce 1 additional unit for free.

factionTech.name: King's Lance
factionTech.ability: Your flagship gains SPACE CANNON 3 (×4) that fires from 2 systems away. This cannot be blocked by Planetary Shield.

flagship.name: The Royal Nova
flagship.ability: When in a system with a stellar anomaly, this ship has combat 4 (×2) and SPACE CANNON 3 (×3). When moving through anomalies, it automatically produces 1 hit against any enemy ships in those systems.

mech.name: King's Champion
mech.ability: On planets in anomaly systems, this unit has combat 4 (×3). BOMBARDMENT 7 (×2).

promissory.name: Royal Favor
promissory.text: When you activate a system: Your ships may move 2 additional systems. Return this card to the Nivyn player.

breakthrough.name: Star Ascension
breakthrough.text: When your flagship enters an anomaly system, immediately deal 2 automatic hits to all enemy ships in that system. Your flagship gains +2 combat for the rest of the round.

agent.name: Royal Astrologer
agent.text: When a player moves ships through an anomaly: You may exhaust this card. Your ships in adjacent systems may also move to that anomaly system for free.

commander.name: Star Admiral
commander.text: Your fleet has +2 combat in all anomaly systems. When you win a combat in an anomaly, gain 1 command token.
commander.unlock: Win 3 combats in anomaly systems.

hero.name: The Star King
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Move your flagship to any anomaly system on the board (ignore movement costs). It immediately fires at all enemy ships in that and adjacent systems.
hero.unlock: Have your flagship in anomaly systems for 4 rounds.

name: The Nokar Sellships
lore: The Nokar Sellships are the galaxy's premier mercenary fleet — a civilization that has turned warfare-for-hire into a cultural institution. Nokar captains are trained from childhood in combat, navigation, and contract law in equal measure, because in their world, a poorly worded contract is more dangerous than enemy fire.

The Nokar do not hold territory for its own sake. They hold it because someone is paying them to, and when that contract expires, they may well take a contract with the other side. This approach has made them simultaneously indispensable and untrustworthy, sought after and feared. The galaxy's most powerful factions all maintain Nokar contracts — and all of them know that every other powerful faction does too.

Their campaign toward Mecatol Rex is, technically, a contract dispute. The Nokar claim that the previous Galactic Council contracted them to "ensure galactic stability" for a period of one thousand years, payment upon completion. The contract expired two centuries ago with the Council's collapse. The Nokar are here to collect what they're owed, with interest.

abilities.name: Mercenary Contract
abilities.text: Action: Choose 1 player and offer them a combat contract (3 trade goods to fight alongside them this round). If accepted, move up to 3 Nokar ships into their active combat. Gain the agreed payment.

abilities.name: Contract Terms
abilities.text: When fighting as a mercenary, your ships gain +1 combat. If the combat is won, gain 1 additional trade good per enemy ship destroyed.

abilities.name: Neutral Colors
abilities.text: While you hold no promissory notes from other players, your ships cannot be targeted first in any combat (enemies must target other ships first).

abilities.name: Fleet Market
abilities.text: Once per Status Phase, you may sell 1 of your ships to another player for any agreed price. The ship permanently joins their fleet.

factionTech.name: Sellship Training
factionTech.ability: Your ships have combat 1 better in any system where you are fulfilling a contract. Contracts are verbal agreements and don't require cards.

factionTech.name: Fleet Insurance
factionTech.ability: When your ships are destroyed while under contract, the contracting player must compensate you 1 trade good per ship destroyed.

factionTech.name: Rapid Deployment
factionTech.ability: When you accept a contract, you may immediately move your ships to the contracting player's system regardless of distance (paying normal command token costs).

factionTech.name: Sellship Elite
factionTech.ability: Your fleet may fight for 2 different players in the same round (in 2 different systems). You gain contract pay from both.

flagship.name: The Contract
flagship.ability: While this ship is under contract, it has combat 4 (×2) and SPACE CANNON 5 (×3). Gain 1 trade good for each hit this ship deals while under contract.

mech.name: Hired Muscle
mech.ability: This unit may be transferred to another player's control for 1 round in exchange for 1 trade good. While under temporary control, it fights for that player.

promissory.name: Nokar Contract
promissory.text: When you activate a system for combat: You may call in the Nokar player. They move up to 3 ships into this combat for free, fighting for you. They gain 1 trade good per enemy ship destroyed. Return this card.

breakthrough.name: Premium Contract
breakthrough.text: When you fight under contract for another player and win, gain 1 VP in addition to normal payment. This can trigger once per game per player you have contracted for.

agent.name: Contract Broker
agent.text: When 2 players are about to fight: You may exhaust this card. Broker a peace deal — both players gain 1 trade good and no combat occurs. You gain 2 trade goods as broker's fee.

commander.name: Fleet Admiral Nok
commander.text: Your ships gain +2 combat while under contract. You may hold 2 simultaneous contracts (fight for 2 players in the same round in different systems).
commander.unlock: Complete 5 contracts.

hero.name: The Master Contract
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Issue a Grand Contract to all players: each player may pay you 3 trade goods to have your entire fleet fight in their defense for 1 round. Accept as many as you choose.
hero.unlock: Earn 15 total trade goods from contracts.

name: The Olradin League
lore: The Olradin League is the galaxy's most persistent and most earnest peace movement. Founded three hundred years ago after a particularly devastating series of interstellar wars, the League began as a diplomatic mission and has expanded into a full civilization-scale project to demonstrate that cooperative governance produces better outcomes than competitive conquest.

The League's territory is structured differently from most factions: they don't control planets so much as steward them, maintaining a policy of minimal military presence and maximum civilian investment. Their systems are among the most prosperous in the galaxy, and other factions have noted with mixed feelings that the League's "soft power" approach to expansion has been remarkably effective at bringing new systems into their fold voluntarily.

They march toward Mecatol Rex not as conquerors but as advocates — with a comprehensive proposal for a reformed Galactic Council, evidence-based policy recommendations, and a well-funded diplomatic corps ready to argue their case before any audience. They have also, reluctantly, brought a fleet, because the galaxy being what it is, sometimes you need to back your arguments with something more persuasive than rhetoric.

abilities.name: Diplomacy First
abilities.text: Before initiating combat, you may offer the opposing player a peace deal (trade goods or promissory notes). If accepted, no combat occurs. Both players gain 1 command token.

abilities.name: Mutual Prosperity
abilities.text: When you make a transaction with another player, both players gain 1 additional trade good. You may make 1 additional transaction per round beyond the normal limit.

abilities.name: League Charter
abilities.text: You may choose to not claim a planet after winning ground combat, instead gaining 2 trade goods and leaving the planet neutral with your protection marker.

abilities.name: Peaceful Expansion
abilities.text: When you move into a system with no enemy ships, you may place 1 Influence token on each planet. Planets with your Influence tokens produce 1 additional influence.

factionTech.name: League Diplomacy
factionTech.ability: Once per Agenda Phase, you may change 1 other player's vote to match yours. That player gains 1 trade good.

factionTech.name: Cooperative Framework
factionTech.ability: When you are allied with another player (mutual promissory notes held), both players' planets produce 1 additional resource.

factionTech.name: Peace Mandate
factionTech.ability: Once per game, declare a Cease-Fire. No player may initiate combat for 1 round. All players gain 1 trade good.

factionTech.name: League Arsenal
factionTech.ability: Your ships gain +1 combat when defending. When defending a planet, your ground forces also gain +1.

flagship.name: The Peace Accord
flagship.ability: When this ship is in a system, all players may make transactions with each other freely in that system. You gain 1 trade good for each transaction made.

mech.name: League Guardian
mech.ability: When this unit is present, no player may initiate BOMBARDMENT against the planet it occupies. PLANETARY SHIELD.

promissory.name: League Endorsement
promissory.text: When you vote on an agenda: Cast 4 additional votes for any outcome you choose. The Olradin player gains 1 trade good per vote cast. Return this card to the Olradin player.

breakthrough.name: Diplomatic Surge
breakthrough.text: When you successfully prevent a combat through diplomacy this round, gain 1 command token and 1 trade good. If you did not initiate any combat this game round, gain 1 extra action card.

agent.name: Ambassador Serin
agent.text: When 2 players are about to enter combat: You may exhaust this card. Both players must attempt diplomacy — offer at least 1 trade good before combat begins. You gain 1 trade good if peace is reached.

commander.name: League Secretary
commander.text: During the Agenda Phase, you cast 1 additional vote for each player who voted the same way as you on the previous agenda.
commander.unlock: Pass 3 agendas unanimously.

hero.name: The Grand Accord
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. All players gain 3 trade goods. No combat may be initiated this round by any player. You score 1 VP for "facilitating galactic peace."

name: The Roh'Dhna Mechatronics
lore: The Roh'Dhna are a species that has, over centuries of deliberate technological self-modification, blurred the line between biological organism and mechanical construct almost entirely. Their bodies are primarily mechanical, maintained and upgraded continuously, with only a small biological core remaining. They view this not as a loss of humanity but as an evolution beyond its limitations.

Their civilization is built around mechatronics — the art of creating systems that seamlessly integrate mechanical and biological components. Their mechs are not vehicles that beings pilot; they are extensions of the beings themselves, connected by neural interfaces so complete that the distinction between pilot and machine has become meaningless.

The Roh'Dhna seek Mecatol Rex because it represents the ultimate engineering challenge: the construction of a galactic administration so efficient that it runs without waste, without redundancy, and without human error. They intend to be the architects of that system, and they are confident that their approach to governance — systems thinking, efficiency optimization, and ruthless elimination of dysfunction — will produce better results than any organic political process.

abilities.name: Mech Supremacy
abilities.text: Your mechs may participate in space combat as well as ground combat. In space, mechs have combat 7 (×2) and count as Fighters for fleet pool purposes.

abilities.name: Neural Interface
abilities.text: Your mechs gain +1 combat for each round they have survived in ongoing combat (cumulative, max +3).

abilities.name: Mechatronic Assembly
abilities.text: When you produce mechs, produce 1 additional mech for free (once per production action).

abilities.name: Integrated Systems
abilities.text: Your mechs count as both Ground Forces and Fighters for the purposes of all abilities and objectives.

factionTech.name: Neural Uplink
factionTech.ability: Your mechs gain ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 8 (×2) and BOMBARDMENT 7. They may fire these in the same round they fight in ground combat.

factionTech.name: Exo-Frame Mk II
factionTech.ability: MECH UPGRADE — Your mechs have combat 4 (×3), Sustain Damage, and +2 movement. They may move between planets within a system freely.

factionTech.name: Fabricator Core
factionTech.ability: When your mechs are destroyed, place 1 replacement mech from your reinforcements on the planet (once per combat). Cost: 1 trade good.

factionTech.name: Apex Mechatronics
factionTech.ability: Your flagship counts as 3 mechs for all ability purposes. Gain 1 trade good for each round your mechs survive in combat.

flagship.name: The Roh'Dhna Prime
flagship.ability: This ship is also a mech for all purposes. It has BOMBARDMENT 5 (×3) and ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 7 (×3). When your mechs win ground combat in this ship's system, they return to full health.

mech.name: Mechatron Elite
mech.ability: This unit fights in space and ground combat. In space it has combat 7 (×2); on ground it has combat 5 (×2). BOMBARDMENT 6. ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 8.

promissory.name: Roh'Dhna Upgrade
promissory.text: Upgrade 1 of your mechs: it gains +2 combat and Sustain Damage for the rest of the game. Return this card to the Roh'Dhna player.

breakthrough.name: Mechatron Surge
breakthrough.text: When your mechs win a combat (space or ground), immediately produce 1 additional mech from your reinforcements in that system for free.

agent.name: Engineer-Prime Roh
agent.text: When a player produces mechs: You may exhaust this card. That player produces 1 additional mech for free. You gain 1 trade good.

commander.name: Apex Integrator
commander.text: Your mechs have +2 combat in all situations. When a mech destroys an enemy flagship, gain 1 VP.
commander.unlock: Have 6 mechs on the board simultaneously.

hero.name: The Grand Integration
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. All your mechs on the board immediately gain +3 combat and Sustain Damage until the end of the round. Produce 2 additional mechs at any Space Dock for free.

name: The Savages of Cymiae
lore: The Savages of Cymiae are misnamed. Yes, they appear primitive by galactic standards — their ships are jury-rigged from salvage, their weapons are modifications of tools, and their social structure looks chaotic to outside observers. But the Cymiae are not primitive; they are adaptive, and the difference is crucial.

They evolved on a world of constant ecological upheaval, where environments shifted dramatically every generation. Civilizations that committed to fixed approaches died; those that could reinvent themselves survived. The result is a species for whom improvisation is not a fallback strategy but the primary mode of operation. No Cymiae commander ever fights the same battle twice.

Their apparent chaos is actually a highly sophisticated doctrine of distributed decision-making: every warrior is trained to make independent tactical choices, and the collective result of thousands of individual improvisations consistently outperforms coordinated plans. The Savages don't have a strategy; they have thousands of strategies running simultaneously.

abilities.name: Adaptive Combat
abilities.text: At the start of each combat, choose 1: your units get +2 combat this round, OR you may move 1 ship from any system to this combat for free, OR you may produce 2 units in this system for free after the combat.

abilities.name: Primal Surge
abilities.text: When your infantry are outnumbered 2:1 or more, they gain +2 combat.

abilities.name: Salvage Doctrine
abilities.text: When you win a combat, gain 1 trade good for each unit lost (yours or enemy) in that combat.

abilities.name: Wild Tactics
abilities.text: Once per round, after rolling dice, you may reroll all dice that did not hit.

factionTech.name: Primal Armor
factionTech.ability: Your infantry have Sustain Damage. When infantry use Sustain Damage, they gain +1 combat until the end of the combat.

factionTech.name: Savage Rush
factionTech.ability: In the first round of any combat, your units produce 1 automatic hit before dice are rolled.

factionTech.name: Wild Engineering
factionTech.ability: When you produce units, you may produce them at any planet you control without a Space Dock (at normal cost). Limit 3 units per production this way.

factionTech.name: Cymiae Berserker
factionTech.ability: Your infantry and mechs gain +1 combat for each round of ground combat that has passed (cumulative, maximum +4).

flagship.name: The Wild Hunt
flagship.ability: This ship may carry 8 infantry. When invading, infantry deployed from this ship get +2 combat in the first round of ground combat.

mech.name: Cymiae Berserker
mech.ability: This unit rerolls all missed dice once per round. BOMBARDMENT 7 (×3). When destroyed, produce 3 infantry on its planet.

promissory.name: Savage Alliance
promissory.text: When you fight a ground combat: Your infantry gain +2 combat for this combat. The Cymiae player gains 1 trade good per enemy unit destroyed. Return this card.

breakthrough.name: Primal Momentum
breakthrough.text: When your infantry win a ground combat, immediately move 2 of your surviving infantry to an adjacent planet. If that planet has enemies, begin another ground combat.

agent.name: War Chief Ghyr
agent.text: When a player initiates ground combat: You may exhaust this card. The attacker may reroll all their ground combat dice once this round.

commander.name: Savage Warlord
commander.text: Your infantry have combat 6 (×2). When you win a ground combat, gain 1 command token.
commander.unlock: Win 6 ground combats.

hero.name: The Great Rampage
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Your infantry on 1 planet may immediately move to all adjacent planets and begin ground combat simultaneously. Win all combats to score 1 VP.

name: The Shipwrights of Axis
lore: The Shipwrights of Axis are the galaxy's premier ship designers and builders, a civilization whose entire culture is organized around the construction of extraordinary vessels. Their home system, Axis, is a massive orbital construction complex that has been in continuous operation for over a thousand years, producing warships of such quality that every major civilization in the galaxy maintains a Shipwright contract.

The Shipwrights view themselves as artists as much as engineers. Each major ship they produce is unique — custom-designed, hand-crafted, and guaranteed. They maintain detailed records of every vessel they have ever built, and they take a professional interest in the performance of their products in combat. Feedback is welcome. Unsatisfactory performance is genuinely distressing to them.

Their campaign toward Mecatol Rex is driven by a proprietary interest: they built a significant portion of the current galactic fleet, and they want to make sure their warranties are honored. Also, they've completed designs for the most ambitious shipbuilding project in galactic history, and they need the resources and political backing that only the galactic capital can provide.

abilities.name: Master Shipbuilder
abilities.text: When you produce ships, reduce their total cost by 2. When you produce a Dreadnought, produce 1 additional Dreadnought for free (once per production action).

abilities.name: Custom Design
abilities.text: Once per game, design a Custom Ship: choose 1 base ship type and add 1 upgrade (SPACE CANNON 5, BOMBARDMENT 5, +2 capacity, or +1 movement). The first ship of this type you build has this upgrade permanently.

abilities.name: Warranty Service
abilities.text: Once per round, you may repair 1 damaged ship in any system you occupy or an adjacent system. Remove 1 damage token.

abilities.name: Flagship Commission
abilities.text: Other players may pay you 4 trade goods to build a special ship in your Space Dock. That ship joins their fleet but was built by you (you may recall it later for 2 trade goods).

factionTech.name: Advanced Drydock
factionTech.ability: Your Space Docks have production capacity 12 and produce 1 additional unit for free per production action.

factionTech.name: Hull Optimization
factionTech.ability: All your ships gain Sustain Damage. Ships with Sustain Damage gain +1 combat while damaged.

factionTech.name: Shipwright Mastery
factionTech.ability: When you produce Dreadnoughts or Flagships, reduce their cost by 2 each. You may produce 1 Dreadnought per round without spending a command token.

factionTech.name: The Grand Design
factionTech.ability: Once per game, build The Grand Design — a super-Dreadnought with combat 4 (×3), 6 capacity, Sustain Damage ×2, BOMBARDMENT 4 (×3), and SPACE CANNON 4 (×3). Cost: 12 resources.

flagship.name: The Masterwork
flagship.ability: This ship has BOMBARDMENT 4 (×3) and SPACE CANNON 4 (×3). When this ship uses Sustain Damage, it repairs itself at the start of the next round.

mech.name: Engineering Mech
mech.ability: This unit may build 1 structure (PDS or Space Dock) on its planet each round without spending a command token. BOMBARDMENT 7.

promissory.name: Shipwright Commission
promissory.text: When you produce ships: You may produce 2 additional ships of the same type for free. Return this card to the Shipwrights player.

breakthrough.name: Production Surge
breakthrough.text: Once per Status Phase, produce up to 3 ships at any Space Dock for half cost (rounded down). When these ships are first deployed in combat, they gain +1 combat.

agent.name: Chief Engineer Varix
agent.text: When a player produces ships: You may exhaust this card. That player produces 2 additional ships for free. You gain 1 trade good per free ship produced.

commander.name: Master Builder
commander.text: Your Dreadnoughts have combat 4 (×3). You may produce 2 Dreadnoughts per round for free.
commander.unlock: Have 6 Dreadnoughts on the board simultaneously.

hero.name: The Grand Fleet
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Produce up to 8 ships of any type at your Space Dock(s) for half cost. Deploy them immediately to any systems you control.
hero.unlock: Produce a total of 25 ships across the game.

name: The Tnelis Syndicate
lore: The Tnelis Syndicate is organized crime elevated to the level of a civilization. What began as a smuggling network three centuries ago has become the galaxy's most comprehensive shadow economy — an organization that operates parallel to legitimate galactic commerce, providing goods and services that official channels won't, can't, or choose not to provide.

Every major civilization in the galaxy has a Tnelis contact. Whether that contact is used for intelligence, forbidden technology, discrete weapons shipments, or simply routing around inconvenient trade regulations depends on the civilization, but the contact exists. The Tnelis maintain careful records of every deal they've ever made, and they have never been known to voluntarily betray a client — the key word being "voluntarily."

The Syndicate's interest in Mecatol Rex is purely practical: controlling the galactic capital would allow them to legitimize their operation, bringing their shadow economy into official structures and converting decades of influence into formal power. They're not interested in governance for its own sake; they're interested in never having to worry about law enforcement again.

abilities.name: Shadow Economy
abilities.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each player who did not make a transaction this round (they "paid protection").

abilities.name: Black Market
abilities.text: Action: Pay 2 trade goods. Gain any 1 technology from the technology deck without paying its cost or meeting prerequisites.

abilities.name: Informant Network
abilities.text: At the start of each round, you may look at any 1 player's action card hand or their secret objective.

abilities.name: Contraband Supply
abilities.text: When you make a transaction with another player, you may supply them with contraband: they gain 2 extra trade goods but must give you 1 of their action cards.

factionTech.name: Syndicate Routes
factionTech.ability: Your ships may move through any system without activating it. You never leave command tokens in systems you pass through.

factionTech.name: Dirty Money
factionTech.ability: Your trade goods count double when used to research technologies or influence agendas.

factionTech.name: Hired Assassination
factionTech.ability: Action: Pay 3 trade goods. Remove 1 ground force (infantry or mech) from any planet in an adjacent system.

factionTech.name: Syndicate Control
factionTech.ability: Once per game, you may declare that you "own" 1 other player's Space Dock. Gain 2 trade goods for each unit produced there this round.

flagship.name: The Shadow
flagship.ability: This ship is never the primary target of enemy abilities — enemies must target other ships first. When this ship wins combat, take 2 trade goods from the losing player.

mech.name: Enforcer
mech.ability: When this unit wins ground combat, the losing player must give you 1 trade good and 1 action card. ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 8.

promissory.name: Tnelis Protection
promissory.text: While you hold this card, no other player may target you with negative abilities (action cards, faction abilities, etc.). In exchange, give the Tnelis player 2 trade goods per round. Return this card at any time.

breakthrough.name: Underground Network
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when another player makes a transaction, gain 1 trade good as a "transaction fee." When you have the most trade goods of any player, gain 1 command token per Status Phase.

agent.name: Syndicate Broker
agent.text: When a player gains a relic or technology: You may exhaust this card. Gain 1 trade good as a "procurement fee."

commander.name: Crime Lord Tnel
commander.text: Your ships are never targeted first in combat. When you win a combat, gain 1 trade good for each enemy ship destroyed.
commander.unlock: Accumulate 20 trade goods at one time.

hero.name: The Syndicate Takeover
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 player. Take control of 1 of their non-home planets immediately. They gain 4 trade goods as "fair market value." This cannot be refused.

name: The Vaden Banking Clans
lore: The Vaden Banking Clans are the galaxy's central financial institution, a family of banking dynasties whose vaults underpin the economies of dozens of civilizations. They don't just hold money; they create money, through a complex system of fractional reserve banking and inter-galactic credit instruments that most other civilizations don't fully understand but depend on absolutely.

The Clans' power comes from this dependency. When the Vaden call in loans, economies collapse. When the Vaden offer credit, wars are won and lost. They have never needed to field a particularly powerful military because their real weapons are financial — the ability to fund their allies' armies and starve their enemies' treasuries simultaneously.

Their interest in Mecatol Rex is existential: the planet's position at the center of galactic trade routes makes it the natural location for the Galactic Central Bank, an institution the Vaden have been planning for centuries. Whoever controls Mecatol Rex controls the galactic financial system, and the Vaden are the only ones who understand that financial system well enough to run it.

abilities.name: Interest Bearing
abilities.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each 3 trade goods you currently hold (compound interest).

abilities.name: Galactic Credit
abilities.text: Action: Offer any player a loan of up to 5 trade goods. If accepted, they must repay double within 2 Status Phases or exhaust 2 planets of your choice.

abilities.name: Market Crash
abilities.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Choose 1 player. They lose 2 trade goods. You gain 1 trade good.

abilities.name: Financial Warfare
abilities.text: When you vote on an agenda, you may spend trade goods to gain extra votes at a rate of 2 trade goods per vote (maximum 5 extra votes).

factionTech.name: Compound Interest
factionTech.ability: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 additional trade good for every 2 trade goods you hold. Gain 1 command token if you hold 10+ trade goods.

factionTech.name: Credit Rating
factionTech.ability: When another player replenishes commodities, you may immediately make a transaction with them (gaining up to 3 commodities for 2 trade goods each).

factionTech.name: Hostile Takeover
factionTech.ability: When you win a space combat, take 1 commodity or trade good from the losing player for each ship they lost.

factionTech.name: Galactic Bank
factionTech.ability: You now earn 1 trade good for each transaction any player makes (including transactions between other players). Gain 1 command token per Status Phase if you hold the most trade goods.

flagship.name: The Treasury
flagship.ability: When this ship is in a system, you may make transactions with any player in the same or adjacent systems. Gain 2 trade goods per round simply for having this ship present.

mech.name: Asset Protection
mech.ability: When this unit is destroyed, the attacking player must pay you 2 trade goods. PLANETARY SHIELD.

promissory.name: Vaden Credit Line
promissory.text: Gain 5 trade goods immediately. You owe the Vaden player 8 trade goods, repayable over the next 3 Status Phases. Failure to pay: exhaust 2 planets per missed payment. Return this card when repaid.

breakthrough.name: Financial Dominance
breakthrough.text: When you have the most trade goods of any player, gain 1 additional VP (this is lost immediately if you no longer have the most). You may spend 5 trade goods to gain 1 VP permanently.

agent.name: Investment Broker
agent.text: When a player produces units: You may exhaust this card. They produce 3 additional units for free but owe you 3 trade goods next Status Phase.

commander.name: Clan Head Vaden
commander.text: At the start of each Status Phase, gain 1 trade good for each player who holds fewer trade goods than you. Your trade good interest rate doubles (1 TG per 2 held).
commander.unlock: Hold 15 trade goods at one time.

hero.name: The Grand Market Crash
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. All other players lose half their trade goods (round down). You gain half of what each player loses (round up). This triggers your compound interest immediately.
hero.unlock: Collect 30 total trade goods from loans.

name: The Vaylerian Scourge
lore: The Vaylerian Scourge is not a civilization in any conventional sense — it is a military machine that has consumed the civilization that created it. What began as a war fleet dispatched to resolve a border dispute centuries ago has become self-perpetuating, expanding by consuming the resources of conquered territories to fuel further conquest, with no end objective except continued expansion.

The original Vaylerian political establishment was dissolved by the military council after a particularly decisive campaign, and the Council has not felt the need to reestablish civilian government since. Their logic is simple: in a galaxy at war, warriors make better decisions than politicians, and the Scourge has not lost a war since the transition.

They advance toward Mecatol Rex because it is the largest unconquered territory in range. The concept of galactic governance is irrelevant to the Scourge's leadership — they don't want to rule the galaxy, they want to consume it. The fact that this amounts to the same thing is a philosophical question they haven't gotten around to addressing.

abilities.name: Scorched Earth
abilities.text: When you win a ground combat, you may destroy all structures on that planet. Gain 1 trade good for each structure destroyed. The planet produces 1 less resource and influence for the rest of the game.

abilities.name: Consume and Expand
abilities.text: After winning a space combat, gain resources equal to the number of enemy ships destroyed. Use these resources immediately to produce units.

abilities.name: Relentless
abilities.text: Your units cannot be forced to retreat. When your fleet is reduced to 1 ship, that ship gains +3 combat.

abilities.name: Scourge Tactics
abilities.text: When you attack with 5 or more ships, gain 1 automatic hit before dice are rolled.

factionTech.name: Scourge Armor
factionTech.ability: Your Dreadnoughts and Carriers gain Sustain Damage. Your ships gain +1 combat when attacking systems they have previously destroyed structures in.

factionTech.name: Consume Protocol
factionTech.ability: After winning space combat, immediately produce 2 ships in the combat system for free (no cost, no command token).

factionTech.name: Scorched Arsenal
factionTech.ability: Your ships have BOMBARDMENT 4 (×3). When you BOMBARDMENT a planet, ignore all Planetary Shields and Sustain Damage abilities.

factionTech.name: Total Scourge
factionTech.ability: Once per game, begin the Scourge Protocol: for 2 rounds, your ships move +1 and have +2 combat. After, restore all consumed planets to normal production.

flagship.name: Vaylerian Prime
flagship.ability: BOMBARDMENT 4 (×4). When this ship wins combat, immediately gain resources equal to enemy ships destroyed and produce up to 3 units.

mech.name: Scourge Destroyer
mech.ability: BOMBARDMENT 6 (×3). When this unit destroys a structure, gain 2 trade goods. When it destroys an enemy mech, gain 1 command token.

promissory.name: Scourge Contract
promissory.text: When you attack a system: The Vaylerian Scourge may join your attack with 3 ships. For each enemy ship destroyed, they gain 1 trade good. Return this card to the Vaylerian player.

breakthrough.name: Unstoppable Tide
breakthrough.text: When you destroy 3+ enemy ships in one combat, gain 1 command token and immediately produce 1 Destroyer from your reinforcements in that system for free.

agent.name: Conquest Commander
agent.text: When a player wins a combat: You may exhaust this card. They may produce 2 units in the combat system for free.

commander.name: Supreme Warlord
commander.text: Your Dreadnoughts have combat 4 (×3). When you destroy a Space Dock, gain 1 VP.
commander.unlock: Destroy 3 enemy Space Docks.

hero.name: The Final Scourge
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. For this round, your ships have +3 combat and BOMBARDMENT 3 (×4). All planets you invade this round cannot be defended (defender's ground forces have -2 combat).

name: The Veldyr Sovereignty
lore: The Veldyr Sovereignty is a civilization built on the principle of absolute self-determination — the belief that no external power has any legitimate authority over them, and that any attempt to exercise such authority is an act of war. This principle extends to every aspect of their governance: no alliances, no obligations, no concessions.

Paradoxically, this extreme independence has made the Veldyr one of the more stable civilizations in the galaxy. Without the entanglements of alliances and the obligations of treaties, they are free to respond to every situation purely on its merits. They have fought wars, made peace, traded, withheld trade, and generally navigated galactic politics with a ruthless pragmatism that other factions, bound by their commitments, can only envy.

The Sovereignty seeks Mecatol Rex for a simple reason: no truly sovereign power can accept the existence of a galactic authority it doesn't control. The Galactic Council represents a claim to authority over the Veldyr that they fundamentally reject. The only solution is to either dissolve the Council or control it, and dissolving it sounds messy.

abilities.text: You are immune to all political agendas that would target you specifically. When a general agenda would negatively affect you, spend 1 influence to negate it.

abilities.name: Self-Sufficient
abilities.text: You gain 1 trade good at the start of each round for each planet you control that has all 3 production types (resource, influence, both) — i.e., systems where you produce both.

abilities.name: Sovereign Pride
abilities.text: Your units gain +1 combat when fighting in your home system. They gain +2 when fighting in any system they have held for 3+ rounds.

abilities.name: Independent Doctrine
abilities.text: You may use the secondary ability of any strategy card without spending a command token (once per strategy card per round).

factionTech.name: Sovereignty Protocol
factionTech.ability: When an agenda would negatively affect you, you may exhaust 1 planet instead of complying. That planet is exhausted but you ignore the agenda's effects.

factionTech.name: Veldyr Armament
factionTech.ability: Your Dreadnoughts and Flagships have +1 combat and +1 movement. They cannot be targeted by enemy special abilities.

factionTech.name: Economic Independence

factionTech.name: The Sovereign Will

flagship.name: The Sovereign

mech.name: Sovereignty Guardian

breakthrough.name: Sovereign Momentum
breakthrough.text: Once per round, when you successfully resist a negative effect targeting you, gain 1 command token and 1 trade good. Sovereign Pride bonus increases to +3 for the rest of the game.

agent.name: Sovereign Voice

commander.name: Grand Sovereign
commander.text: Your units are immune to all enemy faction special abilities. When you are targeted by a negative effect, gain 1 trade good instead.
commander.unlock: Successfully resist 5 negative effects.

hero.name: Declaration of Sovereignty
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Declare absolute sovereignty: for 2 rounds, no player may activate your systems, target your planets, or use abilities against your units. You gain 1 VP.
hero.unlock: Control your home system for 5 consecutive rounds.

name: The Zelian Purifier
lore: The Zelian Purifier is a zealous theocracy built around the belief that the galaxy is corrupt, contaminated by impurity at every level, and must be cleansed before it can be reborn. Their purification theology is comprehensive: impure planets must be sterilized, impure civilizations must be reformed or destroyed, and impure government must be replaced with righteous order.

Critics of the Purifier note that "impurity" has a suspiciously flexible definition that seems to expand to include anything the Purifier finds inconvenient. Supporters note that the Zelian's territory is exceptionally orderly, crime is nonexistent, and productivity is extraordinary. The secret of this productivity — total, enforced compliance — is something the Purifier's promotional materials do not emphasize.

They seek Mecatol Rex because the Galactic Council is, in the Purifier's theological framework, the most profound expression of galactic corruption: a body that attempts to accommodate all beliefs and all peoples, producing the moral relativism that the Zelian consider the galaxy's root disease. They intend to provide clarity. Permanent, absolute clarity.

abilities.name: Purge

abilities.text: Your BOMBARDMENT hits cannot be cancelled by Planetary Shield. When you BOMBARD a planet, you may choose which ground forces take hits.

abilities.name: Righteous Judgment

abilities.name: Holy Purge

factionTech.ability: Your BOMBARDMENT has +2 dice. Purified planets you control produce 1 additional resource.

factionTech.name: Cleansing Beam

factionTech.name: Total Purification
factionTech.ability: Once per game, Purify all planets in 1 system: remove all tokens, attachments, and enemy forces. These planets become Purified and produce double resources for you.

flagship.name: The Cleansing Star

mech.name: Purifier Mech

breakthrough.name: Sacred Cleansing

agent.name: High Purifier
agent.text: When a player wins a ground combat: You may exhaust this card. The winning player may Purify the captured planet, removing 1 attachment. You gain 1 trade good.

commander.name: Grand Inquisitor
commander.text: Your BOMBARDMENT automatically Purifies planets. When you control 5+ Purified planets, gain 1 additional VP.
