hero.name: The Righteous Flame
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Purify every planet in all systems adjacent to your home system. Gain 1 command token for each planet Purified. Score 1 VP.

name: The Glimmer of Mortheus
lore: The Glimmer of Mortheus defies easy categorization. They are what remains of a civilization that died — completely, absolutely, finally — and then came back. Not as ghosts, not as undead, but as something genuinely new: beings who have experienced total annihilation and returned through processes that their own scientists cannot fully explain.

The original Mortheus civilization was destroyed six centuries ago by a weapon of mass annihilation. What returned, decades later, were echoes: partial reconstructions of the original beings, assembled from residual quantum information and ambient cosmic energy. These Glimmers are not the original Mortheus. They have their memories but not their continuity. They died, and what came back is something that remembers dying.

This experience has given the Glimmer a unique perspective on galactic conflict: they have already lost everything, and what remains is not fear of death but a profound curiosity about why they were allowed to return. Mecatol Rex is the center of the galaxy's information network, and the Glimmer believe that somewhere in its archives lies the answer to the question they have been asking for six centuries: who destroyed them, and why did they come back?

abilities.name: Resurrection
abilities.text: When your ships are destroyed, you may immediately return up to 2 of them from your reinforcements to any system you control (once per combat). Cost: 1 trade good per ship returned.

abilities.name: Echo State
abilities.text: Your units that have been destroyed once this game gain +1 combat (they "remember" how they died). Track this with tokens.

abilities.name: Undying Will
abilities.text: Your units in your home system cannot be destroyed by any ability. They may only be removed by winning ground combat against them.

abilities.name: Glimmer Network
abilities.text: At the start of each round, draw 1 action card if any of your units were destroyed last round.

factionTech.name: Mortheus Protocol
factionTech.ability: When any of your units is destroyed, gain 1 trade good. When 3+ units are destroyed in one combat, draw 1 action card.

factionTech.name: Echo Armor
factionTech.ability: All your ships have Sustain Damage. Ships with Sustain Damage that have been used in combat before gain +1 combat.

factionTech.name: Glimmer Surge
factionTech.ability: When you return a unit through Resurrection, it has +2 combat for the rest of the round.

factionTech.name: Full Manifestation
factionTech.ability: Once per game, return all your destroyed units from your reinforcements to the game board (distribute them across systems you control). They have +1 combat permanently.

flagship.name: Mortheus Reborn
flagship.ability: When this ship is destroyed, it returns to your home system next round at full health (it can only be permanently destroyed if your home system is captured). While in play, it grants +1 combat to all your ships in the same system.

mech.name: Glimmer Construct
mech.ability: When this unit is destroyed, return it to any planet you control next round for 1 trade good. It has +1 combat permanently after its first destruction.

promissory.name: Glimmer Blessing
promissory.text: When your units are destroyed: You may immediately return up to 3 of them from reinforcements to any system you control. Return this card to the Glimmer player.

breakthrough.name: Death Defied
breakthrough.text: Once per game, when you lose a space combat and all your ships in a system are destroyed, immediately return all of them at the start of your next turn. They all gain +1 combat permanently.

agent.name: Echo Keeper
agent.text: When a player loses units in combat: You may exhaust this card. That player may return 1 unit from their reinforcements to the combat system immediately.

commander.name: Glimmer Prime
commander.text: Your Resurrection ability costs nothing (no trade good required). When you return a unit through Resurrection, it has +2 combat for the rest of the round.
commander.unlock: Return 5 units through Resurrection.

hero.name: The Undying
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Return ALL your units from your reinforcements to the game board (place them in any systems you control). They all have +1 combat permanently. Gain 1 VP.
hero.unlock: Lose and return from combat 5 times.

name: The Zealots of Rhodun
lore: The Mahact were not the first great galactic empire, nor will the Lazax be the last, so say the Zealots of Rhodun and their annals. A religious order who has traveled between the placid orbits of the Orad system, the Zealots claim to be the galactic keepers of historical record of their origin. They are sworn to protect the technological secret of ancient powers.

When the Rhodun first came to the Orad system, it was in a time when few species had yet traveled between the stars. Their journey was in religious pilgrimage, acting on guidance from their forebears. If the Rhodun keep record of their origins, a time they almost surely do, this information has not been shared with outsiders. This is not atypical; to say that the Rhodun closely guard their knowledge would be a vast understatement.

The Zealots dwell primarily on Orad III, known simply as "Orad", a planet with a highly ritualized and ordered life. On Orad, the Rhodun have living officials of climbing ranks managing high-level technology or labor-intensive tasks being carried out by the Placed, the less-advanced indigenous inhabitants of the planet. Pok, the other habitable planet in the system, is a destination for the original Rhodun pilgrimage; it is now a simple Merchant Temple where only the High Zealots may tread. Pok's lonely spires and sprawling temple complexes are home, it is said, to many of the Zealots of Rhodun's most closely-held secrets.

abilities.name: Conspirators

abilities.name: Ancient Knowledge

factionTech.ability: Once per round, when you research a technology, you may exhaust 1 planet with a technology specialty to reduce that technology's prerequisites by 1 of the matching color.

factionTech.name: Zealot Drive
factionTech.ability: Your ships may use the technology specialties of planets you control in the activated system as if those planets were exhausted.

factionTech.name: Sacred Doctrine
factionTech.ability: When you research a technology, you may spend 1 commodity to ignore 1 prerequisite of any color.

factionTech.name: Orad Archives
factionTech.ability: After you research a technology with 2 or more prerequisites, gain 1 command token.

flagship.name: Reckoning

mech.name: Orad Zealot
mech.ability: When this unit is on a planet with a technology specialty, you may exhaust this card to ignore 1 prerequisite when researching a technology this round.

promissory.name: Rhodun Blessing
promissory.text: When you research a technology: Ignore all prerequisites on that technology. Return this card to the Zealots of Rhodun player.

agent.name: High Priest Vael
agent.text: When a player researches a technology: You may exhaust this card. That player may ignore 1 additional prerequisite. You gain 1 commodity.

commander.name: Bishop Ulin

commander.unlock: Have a technology with 2 or more prerequisites.

hero.name: The Prophet
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. Research 1 technology from the technology deck, ignoring all prerequisites. Gain 1 command token.

name: The Berserkers of Kjalengard
lore: The Kjalengard Berserkers are not merely soldiers — they are an entire civilization built around the philosophy of honorable war. Their culture holds combat as the highest form of expression, not as a means to an end but as a form of competitive integrity. To die in battle is the greatest possible honor; to witness a glorious victory and survive is likewise worthy.

Their moniker of "Berserker" belies the care and thought that goes into Kjalengard warmaking. Tactical deployments of ships and infantry are a hallmark of their victory dance, and Kjalengard command often broadcasts the site of impending battles to their enemies as a form of competitive integrity. Kjalengard fleets will often disarm enemy ships with strategic fire, leaving them to spin helplessly, harmless as witnesses to glorious Kjalengard victory.

The throne-world of Mecatol Rex calls to the Berserkers, a legendary battlefield tarnished by the treachery of a cowardly war. What better place could exist for their people to live and die a life worth telling stories about?

abilities.name: Glory
abilities.text: After you win a combat, you may place or move a Glory token into the active system, or spend 1 token from your strategy pool to research a unit upgrade technology of the same type as 1 of your units that participated in that combat.

abilities.name: Heroism
abilities.text: Capture your fighters and infantry that are destroyed during combat. When you pass, for each Glory token on the game board, you may return 4 of your captured units to gain 1 command token.

abilities.name: Valor
abilities.text: When units make a combat roll in a system that contains a Glory token, each result of 10, before applying modifiers, produces 1 additional hit.

startingTech: 1 non-faction unit upgrade technology of your choice

factionTech.name: Kjalengard Warsong
factionTech.ability: When you produce a hit against an opponent's units, you may spend 1 trade good to capture 1 of the destroyed units instead of removing it from the game.

factionTech.name: Berserker Drive
factionTech.ability: Your ships have +1 movement in systems that contain a Glory token. When you win a combat in a system with a Glory token, gain 1 command token.

factionTech.name: Glory Engine
factionTech.ability: After combat, for each Glory token on the board, your ships in the active system get +1 combat for the rest of the round. Remove all Glory tokens at the end of the Status Phase.

factionTech.name: Ancestral Hearth
factionTech.ability: Once per round, after you capture units, you may immediately return up to 2 captured infantry or fighters to your reinforcements instead of holding them.

flagship.name: Hulgade's Hammer
flagship.ability: After the first round of combat in this system, place up to 2 of your captured units in this system or on that planet.

mech.name: Star Dragon
mech.ability: This unit may ignore the movement effects of non-supernova anomalies. When this unit is destroyed in combat, capture it instead of removing it.

promissory.name: Kjalengard Glory Pact
promissory.text: When you win a combat: Place 1 Glory token in the active system. The Berserkers of Kjalengard player gains 1 commodity. Return this card to the Berserkers player.

agent.name: Merkismathr Asvand
agent.text: At the start of a combat: Exhaust this card to move a Glory token to the active system, if able. Then, the active player may gain a number of commodities equal to the number of neighbors they have.

commander.name: Sdallari Tvungovot
commander.text: When researching a unit upgrade technology, each of your unit upgrade technologies may satisfy 1 prerequisite it shares with the technology you are researching.
commander.unlock: Have 2 Glory tokens on the game board.

hero.name: Ygegnad, The Thunder
hero.text: Action: Exhaust this card. For each system that contains a Glory token, remove up to 1 command token in or adjacent to that system from the game board and gain 1 command token, if able.
hero.unlock: Win 3 combats in systems containing a Glory token.
